We recommended that Management strictly implement City Ordinance No. 2003-007 in conformity with RA No. 9003, particularly:

Waste segregation at source be strongly monitored (The City to partner with barangay personnel to monitor every household);




Schedule of garbage collection with regard to biodegradable, non-biodegradable and recyclables likewise be strictly implemented (The City to strategize techniques and methods like provision of personnel to MRFs or dumping areas to oversee that residents dump wastes that are properly segregated); and

Penalty be strictly enforced to violators.
observations.managementAction:
Page 67 of 2018 AAR | Although Materials Recovery Facilities are maintained for each barangay, waste segregation at source was not practiced. 

Extensive effort is needed to fully implement the ordinance.





The City has yet to enforce penalties for violation of ordinance.
observations.reason:
Strict implementation of the ordinance requiring for waste segregation was not employed by the City. 

Not Implemented.

Regular collection of garbage wastes was performed by the City but collections of biodegradable and non-biodegradable were not separated. The City has not implemented the ordinance providing for schedules of collection of biodegradables and non-biodegradables. 

Not Implemented.

Compliance to City Ordinances pertaining to the imposition of penalties were not strictly adhered to.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the (a) City Accountant to: 

(i) consistently record the purchased inventories to their respective asset accounts; and 

(ii) ensure that the de-recognition of inventories is recorded only when these are issued to end-users; 



(b) City Health Officer to prepare and submit monthly the corresponding control documents such as Stock Cards (SCs) to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the monitoring and accountability for supplies and materials; and 

(c) City General Service Officer to ensure proper safekeeping of supplies and materials inventory to minimize the risk of possible loss or wastage of government resources.

We recommended that the LCE require the Planning Officer to: 

(a) include in their PPAs that will have an impact on health and environment issues to control the spread of the virus; 

(b) provide additional allocation for PPAs for COVID-19 preventive measures in accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and 

(c) continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-19 related  PPAs and assure inclusion  thereof in the AIP in accordance with Item 2.1  DBM DILG JMC No. 02.

We recommended  and the LCE agreed to:

(a) instruct the Chairperson of the POC to use the intended funds  to actively respond to pandemic crisis  by identifying related PPSAs; and 


(b) request the Chief of Police to maximize the use of the POF set as an assistance during the crisis, in accordance with DILG MC No. 2020-079 and DBM LBC No. 124, also known as “Policy Guidelines on the Provision of Funds by Local Government Units for Programs, Projects, and Activities to Address the COVID-19 Situation”.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC officials to: 

(a) exert best effort to obtain lower prices in the procurement of basic goods and medical supplies not only during calamities but also in regular times; 

(b) obtain data on prices of goods procured by other LGU as basis of price comparison in the City’s procurement; and 

(c) adhere with the rules of the Price Act Law, to set an example with other buyers and, if possible, coordinate with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) to hold the hoarders of goods liable for the violation of the said law.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to verify each item under the “Construction in Progress” account and reclassify, if applicable, the completed projects to proper PPE accounts and compute the applicable depreciation, and to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of construction projects in order to enable timely reclassification.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management and the latter agreed to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to observe the proper recording of assets and expenses in the disbursements in the Trust Fund books of accounts in accordance with IPSAS 1 and to record the revenue upon fulfilment of condition in accordance with IPSAS 23.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Expedite the evaluation of collectability of the receivable accounts and exert more efforts in the retrievable of supporting documents;
Issue demand letters to debtors/AOs that are still present and whose address or whereabouts can still be determined; and
Initiate the processing of request for authority to write-off the accounts which are uncertain for collection due to being dormant for more than 10 years or due to the death of the debtors/accountable officers.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management and the latter agreed to require the offices concerned to strictly adhere to the rules on withholding of retention money from every progress payment as required under Annex E of IRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure that uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities are covered and that the infrastructure projects will be satisfactorily done and on schedule.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to require the concerned contractors for each completed project which have been already fully paid as enumerated to post or submit the warranty security for their respective projects.; and
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that warranty security, which forms part of the supporting documents, be posted before final payment of the projects to secure the agency adequate guarantee against structural defects/faults.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to stop the utilization of trust fund for the payment of overtime services without legal basis and to utilize instead the general fund of the LGU subject to the PS limitations provided for by CSC-DBM JC No. 2, s. 2015 and RA No. 7160.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management, through the Local Health Board, to:
Maximize timely utilization of the funds held in trust by the LGU by creating Work and Financial Plan that will be integrated in the investment plans of the LGU and monitor its implementation, and
Strictly abide with the existing laws and Philhealth guidelines on the proper disposition and allocation of the health funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to ensure that all disbursements are sufficiently supported with complete documentation and to submit necessary supporting documents to prove the accuracy and validity of the payments which includes the following:
List of eligible health workers indicating positions, compensation/s entitle to, and days physically present; and
Daily Time Records or other supporting documents to prove the claim.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to require the LDRRMO to:
Prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances together with supporting documents such as Acknowledgment receipts of the donations in-kind, Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, and Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, as required under COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, whenever disaster related donations are received by the LGU; and
Submit the lacking list/s of Distribution of In-Kind Donations and Acknowledgement Receipts of donations received from OCD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Property Officer to analyze the items included in the RPCPPE to exclude non-PPE accounts such as semi-expendable properties, and properly classify each property into its appropriate PPE accounts; and
Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to (i) maintain Supplies and PPE Ledger Cards and Supply and Property Cards, respectively, for each stock of inventory items and PPE; (ii) allot time and effort to prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the inventories and properties and submit the same to the Audit Team; and (iii) strictly comply with the guidelines/procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the purpose of cleaning the enormous amount of discrepancies in the PPE account balances of the Municipality.
During the exit conference, the Management assured the Audit Team to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the:
Municipal Accountant (i) together with the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile thoroughly their respective records and present fairly the Cash accounts balances in the FS; (ii) to prepare CYs 2019 to 2021 BRS and submit the same to the Audit Team, together with the copies of bank statements and supporting schedules of reconciling items; (iii) to submit Subsidiary Ledgers pursuant to Section 2 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010; and (iv) to furnish the Audit Team, with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the receipt of the ₱500,000.00 insured amount from the PDIC for the savings account with the then Rural Bank of Cajidiocan; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Audit Team a copy of Official Receipt issued for the receipt of the ₱500,000.00 insured amount.

The Management commented that the record keeper of the subsidiary ledger (Data Controller) is conducting already a reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the cash account balances. Also, the Municipal Accountant committed that the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the corresponding cash subsidiary ledgers for CYs 2019 to 2021 are available for submission. Further, he commented that they will look for the copy of JEV on the receipt of the insured amount of ₱500,000.00 and submit the same to the Audit Team once available. While the Municipal Treasurer remarked that it will be a tough task to locate the documents needed such as bank statement and passbook as a proof of the receipt of the insured amount since the closure of then Rural Bank of Cajidiocan occurred in 2007, nevertheless, she committed to submit the required document once she returned to the office after her official travel.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper recording of the income and discount recognized from RPT in the books of accounts, and henceforth ensure to revisit the provisions provided in the MNGAS, Volume I, and R.A. No. 7160, to recognize the correct net income in the consolidated FS;
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies in intensifying the collection of real property tax such as an information campaign to encourage taxpayers to take advantage of early payment discount; and
enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records thoroughly and present the correct amount of tax delinquencies to establish the reliability of accounts in the FS, and likewise ensure that the balances of RPT and SET Receivables are reconciled, at all times.

The Management commented thru the Municipal Accountant that they will review their practice in the preparing of JEV for the RPT collections and will initiate correcting entry/ies as needed.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review and investigation to determine the cause of abnormal balances, and initiate corrections/adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the accounts, and thereafter, to conduct periodic reconciliation of his records to ensure the accuracy of reported data and balances in the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant commented that their Data Controller is conducting a review of the accounts with abnormal balances and assured to prepare adjusting entry/ies as required.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the BAC to (i) explain/ and or justify the noted lapses and deficiencies in the conduct of procurement, particularly those procured under reimbursement basis, submit all lacking bid documents to the Audit Team, and henceforth, take note of the additional observations and adhere strictly to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of the procurement process and ensure that all bid documents are complete, evaluated, and submitted on time; (ii) use the prescribed bidding documents and standard forms in the sixth (6th) edition of PBD and GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 dated September 16, 2020; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing claims for payment of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and submit to the Audit Team the noted documentary deficiencies relative to the procurement of COVID-19 supplies in CY 2021.

The Management commented that they will comply with the above audit recommendations.

With that, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the BAC and its BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team all lacking bid documents noted in the above procurement of infrastructure projects and goods;
require the Municipal Engineer to provide the necessary and approved Time Extension for projects Expansion of Potable Water System Level III and Construction of Slaughter House to substantiate the changes in project duration and expiry date of the contracts; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) impose liquidated damages against the contractors of the above two projects should the approved Time Extension will not be submitted; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the above-enumerated lacking documents and ensure that all disbursements shall be properly supported with documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (iii) ensure that supplies or materials are recorded under proper inventory accounts upon purchase following the perpetual inventory method of accounting, expenses shall be recognized only upon issuance to the end-users, and costs incurred during construction and project completion shall be recorded in the appropriate asset accounts, accordingly.

The Management commented that they will comply with the above audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to prepare a correct and updated LDRRMFIP and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and ensure reconciliation thereof to ensure accuracy and completeness of the information contained on their respective reports; (b) BAC to submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking bid documents in the procurement of one unit Transport Vehicle by Sea amounting to ₱9,985,000; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the details of the unexpended balance of the Municipality’s LDRRMF for the current year as well as that of the previous years are properly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

The MDRRMO assured the Audit Team to use the updated form of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to ensure the accuracy and completeness of DRRM information. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant committed to include the details of the DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements and guaranteed that all documentary requirements are complete before processing any claims and will submit the lacking documents noted in the course of audit.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to prepare a correct and updated LDRRMFIP and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and ensure reconciliation thereof to ensure accuracy and completeness of the information contained on their respective reports;
BAC to submit to the Audit Team the above-mentioned lacking bid documents in the procurement of one unit Transport Vehicle by Sea amounting to ₱9,985,000.00; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the details of the unexpended balance of the Municipality’s LDRRMF for the current year as well as that of the previous years are properly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

The MDRRMO assured the Audit Team to use the updated form of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to ensure the accuracy and completeness of DRRM information. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant committed to include the details of the DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements and guaranteed that all documentary requirements are complete before processing any claims and will submit the lacking documents noted in the course of audit.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of the ongoing PPAs to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that satisfactory benefits of the development projects shall be delivered to the intended beneficiaries, and likewise disclose the above-enumerated projects in the quarterly Report of Government PPAs to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation thereof.
During the exit conference, the Management assured the Audit Team to comply with the audit recommendations.
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office to trace down the breakdown of unremitted balance of taxes withheld in prior years and effect adjusting entry/ies, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
update its records and determine the components of unidentified PPE items reported in the books; and
record all acquisition, disposal or transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.
Municipal Treasurer in her capacity as Property Custodian in coordination with the Inventory Committee if already created to:
conduct an annual physical inventory of all PPE items, including local road networks;
update its records and prepare RPCPPE and RPCLRN following the format prescribed in Annex 49 of NGAS for LGUs, and Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008, respectively; and
prepare and maintain PCs for each class of PPE to record the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information about the asset.

We recommended that the LGU, through the Accounting Department: (a) restate the reported balances of the Welfare Goods for Distribution inventory account for CY 2020 in the Statement of Financial Position, Welfare Goods Expenses account for CYs 2020 and 2021 in the Statement of Financial Performance, and Prior Period Adjustments for CY 2020 in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity; and (b) provide a note disclosure for the prior period adjustments, in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management:
together with the Municipal Development Council, to ensure that PPAs in the Local Development Plan are supported with the necessary in-depth study and planning documents before its inclusion in the Plan to warrant the possible attainment of the project within the year.
require the MPDC and implementing offices to consider during their deliberation the possible limitations in the implementation of the proposed priority projects, and ensure that these projects are realizable within the year it was intended to be completed;
direct the implementing offices to address the limitations or problems identified during the planning phase to guarantee the completion of the projects and deliver its purpose to the intended beneficiaries; and
make a cost benefit analysis in determining the most appropriate mode of implementation, and consider implementing the projects by contract if it will resolve the issues on manpower so as not to deprive the constituents and the community of the optimum and timely benefits that could be derived from these projects.

We recommended that the Management:
revert back to the General Fund, and make available for the provision of social services identified by the local Sanggunian the unexpended balance of DRRMF in CY 2015 of ₱1,125,000.00;
include the unexpended DRRMF for the past five years in its annual LDRRMFIP to fund the continuation of partially implemented and unimplemented DRRM PPAs, and other identified PPAs by the LGU under the four thematic areas of DRRM; and
Increase the utilization rate of DRRMF through proper and effective implementation of planned PPAs.

We strictly recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the use of appropriated funds thru the preparation of the Registries of Appropriations (RAO) which shall be maintained by Fund class, Expense class (PS, MOOE, CO), per Office and per Special Accounts;
Municipal Budget Officer to examine or check anytime the balance of the appropriations and the recording in the registries before signing the certification on the existence of available appropriated funds in the appropriate box in the Disbursement Voucher.
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the basis in the preparation of SCABAA which is the SAAOB are reconciled with their record to properly serve its purpose, which is to reflect the LGUs execution of budget.
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that cash books are updated daily, the Cash in Bank balances are monitored properly, and disbursement levels are checked periodically to determine its availability to support the expenditure.
We likewise recommended that Management refrain from incurring expenditures without assurance that it was properly covered with appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the Municipal Treasurer adopt/enroll in an Automated Payroll System to facilitate the immediate and direct crediting of salaries and other personnel benefits to the individual bank accounts of employees and job orders, and eliminate the risk of loss or misappropriation of public funds that are inherent in a manual system.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of major repairs of the LGU Main Building in the amount of ₱3,493,230.10 from the Repairs and Maintenance-Building and Other Structures account to the Building and Other Structures account, and subject the same to proper depreciation, in order to better reflect the expected use of the asset.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts, and immediately take up the adjustments in the books of accounts; and
conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to ensure that discrepancies are immediately identified, reconciled and adjusted.
In addition, to better illustrate the nature of intra-agency transactions, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
reclassify to, and use the accounts Due from Special Accounts and Due to Special Accounts for fund transfers between accounts belonging in the same fund;
set-up simultaneously the receivable account in the General Journal (GJ) of the Receiving Fund with the recording of the payable account in the GJ of the Source Fund for succeeding fund transfers pertaining to withheld taxes for remittance to BIR; and
reverse the entry made in the GJ pertaining to the setting up of a payable account amounting to ₱1 million.
We also recommended that checks representing fund transfers involving the different accounts of the LGU be named after the account name of the receiving account to ensure its immediate, direct and correct deposit.

We recommended that Management release these warranties to the concerned suppliers or contractors when the following conditions exist:
for procurement of goods, after the lapse of the warranty period, or in the case of expendable supplies, after consumption thereof; Provided, however, That the supplies delivered are free from patent and latent defects and all the conditions imposed under the contract have been fully met; or
for procurement of infrastructure projects, after the lapse of one (1) year from the date of issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the Procuring Entity.

We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to:
maximize the utilization of GAD funds and monitor implementation of GAD PPAs so that no activity is left unimplemented at the end of the year;
prepare a GAD Accomplishment Report based on the GAD Plan and Budget previously reviewed and endorsed by DILG, fill out columns therein with appropriate and adequate information, and attach relevant supporting documents; and
implement a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Environmental Management Specialist to review and update annually the provisions of the 10-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality to reflect the true conditions of its program and to effectively address the current situation of its components;
ensure compliance to the requirements of the DENR-EMB V in the development of the Solid Waste Management Plan and of the DENR-MGB V to facilitate the assessment of the proposed sanitary landfill; and
work closely with the officials of the barangays to ensure that the Municipal policies on solid waste management are properly communicated and implemented at the barangay level to ensure that their solid waste management practice are supportive of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
Establish an Inventory Committee to facilitate conduct of physical count of all Municipality’s PPE and submit the corresponding RPCPPE. Thereafter, during the actual count, the type, description, serial number, acquisition date, name/designation of employee accountable, location and condition of the property counted must be indicated in the Inventory Sheet;
Document the issuance of PPE to end-users through PAR to establish custodianship and assign accountability; and
Require the Municipal Accountant prepare the lapsing schedule of all PPE to properly determine the correctness and validity of reported depreciation expenses and carrying value of PPE.
We also reiterated our recommendation that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to maintain Stock and Property Cards in accordance with Section 119 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the result of the actual physical count of PPE with the required PPE records to establish correctness and validity of the account balances presented in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the BRSs pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and prepare SLs per bank account. A revised copy of the general journal must be immediately submitted to the Office of the Auditor for us to validate the adjustments made to the Cash in Bank account;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to close the accounts that have become dormant and revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case maybe, if the said accounts represent excess of the expenses for the PPAs funded by NGAs and/or other Agencies.
Consider maintaining only one bank account for GF which contains local income collections and IRA of the LGU for a stronger internal control and convenience in the disbursement process.

We recommended that the Accounting Office:
Prepare and maintain SLs as many as necessary for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine the correctness of account balances at any given period; and
Use the available data in the accounting records as beginning balances of the SLs to facilitate preparation and maintenance thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management facilitate immediately the request for write-off of the aforementioned account balances so that the same will be derecognized in the Financial Statements, subject to the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor all paid DVs for CY 2020 still in their possession, (ii) ensure submission of paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month; and (iii) avoid voluminous submissions of DVs pertaining to transactions for several months all at once; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs and official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month.
We also recommended that Management strictly observe the check disbursement process prescribed by Section 44 of the MNGAS in order to accelerate the submission of the DVs to the Office of the Auditor and to prevent accumulation of DVs without supporting documents in the Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management:
Avoid the practice of charging personnel services expenditures to 20 per cent DF so that development programs and projects envisioned by the government are properly implemented; and
Ensure that development projects included under the 20 percent DF are those that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to promotion of the general welfare of the people.

We recommended that Management require the concerned official to submit copies of perfected contracts and POs together with the complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after execution for purposes of review thereof and technical evaluation by COA TAS, as the case may be.

We recommended that Management:
Direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who were unable to comply with the requirement;
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee with existing unliquidated cash advance. However, if it is necessary to grant cash advances for special activities, Management must grant the same to the designated Special Disbursing Officer; and
Enforce granting of cash advances only to those who are duly bonded to afford adequate protection to the government and strictly comply with Item 7.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also reiterate our recommendation that Management immediately stop the practice of granting CAs to its employees without legally authorized specific purpose.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed rules on proper recording of RPT and SET Receivables under Section 248 of RA No. 7160 and Section 20, Volume I of NGAS for LGUs; and
The Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in improving the collection efficiency of the Municipality by posting the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax at the main entrance of Municipal Hall and other publicly accessible place and conspicuous place in each barangay as required under Section 254 of RA No. 7160 or mailing demand letters to its constituents individually in order to compel them to settle their tax liability.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC to facilitate immediate implementation of the transferred funds of LGSF-DRRAP before December 31, 2022. Otherwise, any unutilized amount must be reverted back to the National Treasury, per NADAI dated February 26, 2021 issued by the Bureau of Treasury;
Ensure that the received grant is only implemented for priority programs and projects enumerated in Section 1 of LBC No. 134; and
Prepare the Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of LGSF-DRRAP and thereafter post the same in the LGU website and in at least three conspicuous places within the locality, upon utilization of the grant.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket in accordance with existing regulation for transparency and accountability over the use of such government motor vehicles; and
Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the Commission on Audit (thru the Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to initiate the transfer of the unutilized balance of LDRRMF in the GF, at the end of the year, to the TF-LDRRM bank account;
Require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the following journal entries relevant to transfer of unutilized LDRRMF in case there is no actual cash transfer during the year:
Transfer of the current year unutilized balance without actual cash transfer:
GF
Dr. Transfers of Unspent DRRMF to the Trust Fund xxx
Cr. Due to Other Funds				xxx
TF
Dr. Due from Other Funds			xxx
Cr. Trust Liabilities-DRRMF			xxx
Entry in the succeeding year for the actual transfer of cash of prior year unutilized balance:
GF
Dr. Due to Other Funds			xxx
Cr. Cash In Bank				xxx
TF
Dr. Cash in Bank			xxx
Cr. Due From Other Funds			xxx
Moreover, SLs for the accounts must be maintained so that annual transfer from the GF to the TF could be monitored and recorded. Likewise, the Accounting Office must exercise diligence in the recording of financial transactions to achieve fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements; and
Ensure that only the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE are transferred to the Special Trust Fund while the unexpended balance of MF-CO must be made continuing in the GF books until the projects funded therefor are completed.

We recommended that Management be more prudent in the utilization of its LDRRMF, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund and require the:
MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP and utilize the LDRRMF for tangible projects and activities that will help build disaster resilient communities and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality, rather than activities for administrative purpose; and
Municipal Budget Officer to include PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP in the preparation of RAAO to check whether an obligation request related to LDRRM is included in the LDRRMFIP.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the timeline for submission of Financial Reports and Financial Statements in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management enforce strict compliance with the provisions of IMA especially the schedules and timeline of implementation of the activities/project so that socio-economic benefits could be derived therefrom by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the execution of the required Deeds of Donation between the Municipal Government of San Fernando and the Provincial Government of Bukidnon and make adjusting entries to correct the recording of two projects amounting to ₱29.699 million pending its approval in order to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to correct the recording of transfers of PPE accounts amounting to ₱6.958 million pending approval of the Sanggunian Bayan and the receiving Barangays and to ensure that all recorded transfers of assets are duly approved in accordance with Section 381 of Republic Act 7160 in order to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the purchased DRR-related Equipment in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2019-71 and IPSAS 23 to present fairly the balance of the PPE account.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to verify each item charged under the “Trust Liability – DRRMF” account and to prepare a Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) together with its supporting documents including the working papers for the correcting entries and submit to the Audit Team for further verification.

We also recommended to the management to ensure that proper accounting treatment must be adapted to financial transactions relating to “Trust Liability – DRRMF” Account to establish strong inventory control mechanism of the LGU.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:

Exert more efforts in the retrieval of prior years’ records to identify the accountable officers/employees/debtors or concerned agencies liable for the unaccounted balance of ₱1.639 million; and

Send demand letters to the identified accountable officer/employee or recipient agency of other receivables.
observations.reason:
Receivables decreased to P4,802,563.42 but the unaccounted balance of P1.639 Million pertaining to CY 2010 and below transactions were still not retrieved.

Demand letters were issued to LGU employees but the AOs that are not connected with the LGU were not yet issued with demand letters

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accounting Office to verify each item under the “Motor Vehicles” account and if warranted, reclassify erroneously recorded items to the proper PPE accounts including the accumulated depreciation.

observations.reason: NB: Vouchers for CY 2021 totalling P5,058,612.42 were not yet submitted as of March 24, 2022

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to the management to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the disbursement vouchers and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording.

Require the Municipal Accountant to account the unrecorded disbursement voucher upon receipt, and submit the same to the audit team for audit and custody.

We recommended to the management to:

Submit justification for the payment of PBB to employees and officials with unliquidated cash advances.;

Require the HRMO, in cooperation with the Municipal Accountant, to strictly observe the guidelines in determining eligible individuals in the granting of Performance-Based Bonus.; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand immediate liquidation of long-outstanding cash advances and implement strict observance of the prescribed period in liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended to the management to:

Formulate internal policy in the payment of mobile charges on top of the monthly subscriptions and to require the concerned employees to personally shoulder the items that are not necessary in the performance of their duties.

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand submission of all pages of monthly bills and the previous billing statements in case there are arrears and to exclude in the payment of monthly bills all items that, upon review, are not necessary in the performance of duty.

We recommended to the management to submit the Certified List of beneficiaries as defined under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 14 which will be the basis for the determination whether only those determined by the LGU as eligible for ESP were actually paid. Also, we maintain our recommendation to submit authorization letters for beneficiaries whose cash subsidies were received by other individuals.

We recommended to the management to ensure that subsequent purchases of goods are supported with Activity / Project Designs and require the GSO to support all issuances for distribution to end-users/beneficiaries with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and maintain complete records of the same as required under COA Circular 2014-002 to ensure that all the goods procured and received by the LGU for social amelioration were actually and fairly received by intended families.

We recommended to the management to ensure the efficient utilization of the LDRRM Fund in order that the objective of the various programs, projects and activities funded therefrom are achieved.

	We also recommended to the management to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to improve planning and budgeting capabilities so that PPAs under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) would not be described in generic terms and prioritize the development projects that are workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and completion, thus attaining the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in an efficient and effective manner.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to issue official receipts for receipts of inter-fund transfer and include them in the Report of Collections and Deposits to be submitted to the accounting office to ensure proper recording of such transactions in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
Set-up Other Receivables account to consider the cost recovery of the site development and water system and derecognize Trust Liability account for accumulated collections thereof.
Other Receivables			1,848,042.00

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Department Heads, with the assistance of the City Accountant and Budget Officer, to coordinate closely among themselves to ensure that PPAs are assigned with proper accounts and to refer to the revised Chart of Accounts so that the correct account titles are assigned for each of their program, project, and activities to be incorporated in the Annual Budget.
Also, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer to revisit the CY 2022 budget to ensure that proper accounts are assigned to PPAs, otherwise, request for a supplemental budget to revise the current year approved budget.
Additionally, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the aforementioned misstatements in the financial reports as follows:
Thereafter, for her to strictly account transactions with the appropriate account title as recommended in the revised chart of accounts for LGUs as provided in COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
The City DRRM Officer to appropriately assign account titles and numbers to the Programs, Projects, and Activities during budget preparation to facilitate proper authorization of obligations and recording of transactions;
The City Budget Officer to obligate transactions based on the assigned account numbers under each of the PPAs implemented and not on the nature of the transaction presented in the Purchase Request. Also, for them to account separately balances of the current appropriation as Continuing and Special Trust Fund; and
The City Accountant to refrain from treating savings and unimplemented Capital Outlays as special trust fund and ensure that only the unspent Quick Response Fund and DRRM-Maintenance and Other Operating Expense are recognized as trust fund. The Statement of Comparison of Budgets and Actual Amounts should also be adjusted as follows:
Lastly, to prepare the following entries to rectify the errors made:
General Fund:
Due from Other Funds				4,931,882.69
