observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the Motor Vehicles in the books of the Municipality and to revisit, as a guide, the illustrative entries provided by COA Circular 2012-002 for procurement of equipment/vehicles under the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Still unrecorded as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

stop the practice of obligating transactions for services not yet rendered, particularly the labor component of projects by administration which are chargeable against funds under continuing appropriation.; and

strictly adhere to the provisions and guidelines set forth for the recording of Accounts Payable under PPSAS 19 and COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No accounts were set up for services not yet rendered for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

record the adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts;
review the composition of balances of the Advances to Contractors account and reconcile the same for each contractor to warrant that the advance payment and the corresponding recoupment are properly recorded; and
maintain separate subsidiary ledger for each contractor with advance payment for the mobilization cost for easier monitoring of balances.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Still remain unadjusted as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to revert back the stale checks to Accounts Payable account by debiting the Government Equity Account and crediting the former.

observations.recommendation:

the Municipal Assessor to review and update the assessment record and seek the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer in reconciling their records to establish a complete and accurate list of taxpayers and the expected receivables due from them;

the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant, based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ index card, a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

the Accountant upon receipt of the certified list from the Municipal Treasurer, record the expected revenue to be realized from the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax at the beginning of each year.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
For this year RPT and SET receivables were still based on estimates.

Unexpended balance for CY 2018 was not transferred at year end while unutilized balances after 5 years were not reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the GF.

limit the number of participants to the attend the same seminar, trainings and conventions which are held out of town/region. For conventions, priority should be given to awardees and officers. 

take into consideration the limited resources available and focus on more important programs of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Attendance to local conventions were limited to a few participants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require an allocation of one percent (1%) for Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) Programs in the Municipality’s Annual Budget for the ensuing year.

(b.)  expedite the creation of solid waste management board that will be in charge of the operation of the SWMP.

Initial steps were undertaken by management:

direct the personnel responsible for the various inventory accounts to require end-users to acknowledge the receipt of the inventories requested and require the adequate monitoring of issued and balances of inventories.

We also remind the Property Officer, being the person responsible for the custody and issuance of government properties, to faithfully observe the regulations on the issuance of government properties.

observations.reason: The files for AREs for previous years were not available when requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. similar to the previous years, the Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget appropriation 5% for the Gender and Development (GAD) programs/activities as required under the above-stated circular.  If the budget is incorporated or streamlined in other offices of the LGU, the appropriation should be marked as GAD related activities for proper identification and monitoring; b. the Mayor to direct the Focal Point Person, to comply with the mandates of the above-mentioned provisions by preparing a GAD Plan to embrace all GAD activities for the year, and the accomplishment report at the end of the year that will prove compliance to said mandate; and c. the Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Point Person to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the above-stated joint circular.
observations.managementAction: GAD was incorporated in some activities of the LGU.
observations.reason: Accomplishment for GAD was mainstreamed with other activities of the Municipality. From the report submitted the execution of the GPB was more than the required 5% allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a budget for enrollment of indigent senior citizens of the Municipality for Philhealth membership be appropriated by Management in accordance with the mandatory requirement set forth in Article 20, Section 2 of the Expanded Senior Citizens Act of 2010.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Still for the year under audit no budget was appropriated by the LGU for the Philhealth membership of indigent senior citizen.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all members of the BAC re-orient themselves of their functions provided for in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.  Compliance to the RIRR of RA No. 9184 is the mandated duty of all the members, non-compliance or ignorance thereof are not valid excuses since the RIRR was already given wide national dissemination. The above practice should not be committed again.  

We also recommended that the BAC, Secretariat, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Mayor should be cautious in the preparation of the bidding documents and other attachments in the procurement of goods.

Require the immediate submission of the print outs of the PhilGEPS postings for the two biddings as well as letters of invitation to the Audit Team and the two other mandated observers.

Moreover, the Audit Team also suggested additional manpower in the Accounting Department to help perform accounting duties so that the Municipal Accountant can devote time to prepare the bank reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all collectors be required to turn-over daily and intact all their collections for the day to the liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer and deposit intact all collections daily or the next banking day to authorized depository bank pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAs.
We also recommended to immediately prepare and send demand letters to the former and current Accountable Officers/Collecting Clerks requiring them to fully settle/turn-over the balance of their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Immediate turnover of collections by the Collecting Clerks to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Delay in the turn over to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund to ensure that the unspent balance is readily available for relief and recovery projects and activities within the next five (5) years. Proper accounting entries must be effected to recognize the transfer to be recorded in the General Fund and Trust Fund books.

We recommended that:

Management in granting financial assistance require the recipient to comply with the documentary requirements provided under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the funds are released. Execution of a Memorandum of Agreement must be done as this contains the stipulation of how the financial assistance will be implemented by the beneficiary; and

The Andres Clemente Jr. National High School be required to liquidate immediately the fund transferred as of December 31, 2017 and to promptly liquidate fund transfers after the completion of each project or activity.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the trust collections due to other government agencies. Prompt remittance of trust collections to other government agencies concerned must be observed to allow the latter of the timely use of these resources for productive economic programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Immediately remit all what is to be remitted and coordinate with the GSIS/Philhealth/Pag Ibig to reconcile figures if it was really existing and effect necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Part of the unremitted balance of due to GSIS account is from previous years. The Municipal Government of San Fernando have entered into a memorandum of agreement with the GSIS. The unremitted balance is the result of untimely remittance that accumulated over the years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. Roles and responsibilities could have been acted upon, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS must submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities be also aligned with the GAD plan per COA Circular 14-001 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

Lastly, the 5% allotment for GAD budget be followed to maximize GAD projects and activities.

We recommended that the Management require the Local Development Council (LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the trust collections due to other government agencies. Prompt remittance of trust collections to other government agencies concerned must be observed to allow the latter of the timely use of these resources for productive economic programs and activities. Trace out the records of Due to Other GOCCs accounts without supporting schedule.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following courses of action:
a. Commence the conduct actual physical inventory taking in accordance with the guidelines provided in Handbook on Property & Supply Management System and invite the COA Auditor to witness the same.
b. Submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year. 

c. Require the Accounting to provide a supporting schedules for each PPE and inventory account in the FS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | The Municipal Inventory and Disposal Committee will allot time to conduct physical inventory on those items that are not yet physically counted. The Municipal Accounting Office together with the Municipal General Services Office will reconcile and exert more effort to identify the unaccounted Property, Plant, and Equipment.
observations.reason: There are some properties that are not physically counted such as waste materials or the disposed properties at the motorpool. The Municipal Accounting Office has still Properties, Plants, and Equipments that has no cost value and unidentified.

Secured the patents of 4 properties in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor’s Office already started the processing of the titling of land properties of the municipalities. But due to some requirements and other issues that need to be taken care of, the processing is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payroll and collection reports in consistent with the existing rules and regulations and in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Submitted already most of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Not yet submitted some disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Accounting Unit to provide correct cash balances by reconciling noted negative balances and proper monitoring of the subsidiary ledgers. Submit detailed explanation to the Audit Team for the negative balances.

We also recommended to request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the 20% development fund.

We recommended that the management require the Accountable Officer (AO) to make a written explanation on the noted deficiency. We recommended further that the Municipal Treasurer together with the liquidating officer closely monitor the daily remittance of the collections of the AO and ensure that the  internal control policies concerning the withdrawals of accountable forms - by the AOs (with unremitted balances) should be strictly followed.

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

•	Submit authority of the Petty Cash Custodian to handle the PCF, indicate the purpose of the Fund.
•	Immediately stop the current practice in recording transactions involving petty cash fund and strictly follow the prescribed imprest system;
•	Refrain from using PCF for payment of financial assistance, instead designate a Special Disbursing Office to Cash Advance for the purpose of payment to financial assistance.
•	Ensure that sufficient internal control is in place by requiring quarterly reconciliation of the Petty cash book and petty cash on hand.

Require the:

•	Human Resource Management Officer to issue a memorandum to require the concerned employees to sign their DTRs that are on-file at the HRMO and create a policy to avoid the lapses on the accomplishment and submission of DTRs; and
•	Accountant to submit all the paid payrolls and their supporting documents, including the months of October 2017 through December 2017 and ensure that all claims against the government are properly supported with all the documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Submitted already most of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Not yet submitted paid payrolls and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing and granting of applications for terminal leave benefits by its officials and employees.

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9344 and appropriate at least one percent (1%) of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of PPAs of the LCPC and utilize the said allocation pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.

We recommended that before joining trainings and seminars organized by a private training institution, the Management should verify first if the organizing body is an accredited institution of the Civil Service Commission or Government Procurement Policy Board in case of the seminars involving RA 9184 to ensure that the agency will be spending government funds to trainings and seminars that meet Qualification Standards.

We recommend that the management abide with the guidelines on procurement provided by RA 9184 to avoid the possible audit disallowances and to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement process.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned office and officials to ensure the full implementation of the projects funded by the 20% development fund to speed up development and progress and eventually to attain the desired outcomes of the projects and to enable the constituents to enjoy their full benefits.

We further recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to demand for liquidation of previous fund transfer from the concerned barangay officials.

We also recommended to require the Local Development Committee to prioritize the development projects that are workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and completion, thus attaining the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in an efficient and effective manner.

observations.reason: There are several projects from prior years that needs to be implemented.

At present, the Assessor’s Office together with the Treasurer’s Office coordinate their efforts on the accurate capture of complete list of taxpayers. The two offices are still in the process of reconciling the data which will be used for the new system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) City Administrator/Local Chief Executive (i) render written explanation on the absence of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the City and its component barangays for the subject financial assistance transactions; (ii) consider granting the financial assistance to barangays, especially those lacking in technical competencies, in completed project form instead of in cash thus making the City as the implementer and the barangay as the beneficiary; and iii) ensure that a MOA between the City and its barangays are executed before a transfer of funds/projects is facilitated; and (b) Accounting Office (i) require the implementing heads of barangay to: (i.a) exhaust all efforts to coordinate with the previous Barangay Administration to facilitate the submission of liquidation documents for the long overdue unliquidated financial assistance;  (i.b) religiously submit all liquidation documents to the City Accounting Office for all financial assistance received;  (i.c) immediately refund the unutilized balance of the financial assistance to the City Treasurer’s Office after the purpose of the grant is completed; and (i.d) strictly observe the proper procedures in the liquidation of financial assistance and ensure completeness of documents upon submission to the accounting personnel in-charge of the books of the barangay; (ii) ensure that all required liquidation documents for previously granted financial assistance have been submitted and issue a Certification that funds previously transferred to the Implementing Agency (IA) have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before processing the grant/transfer of another financial assistance; (iii) closely monitor the compliance of the recipient barangays on the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period; and (iv) draw journal entries to recognize all settlements already complied with by the barangays concerned relative to the prior years’ financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred with our recommendations and agreed to exert maximum efforts to facilitate the submission of liquidation documents for long overdue unliquidated financial assistance granted to barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) review or analyze all outstanding payables to pave the way for the (i) de-recognition in the books of all obligations of the City that had already been extinguished thru payment, cancellation or other means; and (ii) reversion  to the General Fund those which had been outstanding for more than two years and without actual claims, administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected contracts or records; (b) follow the check disbursement process described in Section 44 of the NGAS Manual and ensure that recognition of accounts payable is based on valid claims supported with adequate documentation and approved DVs; (c) maintain an aging of accounts payable to facilitate proper monitoring of the movement of the Accounts Payable; and (d) post entries on a per transaction basis in the  subsidiary ledger to allow for matching of payment with the recorded payables and timely de-recognition or adjustment of excess amount of payables arising from premature recognition of contract amounts as payables.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that they would look into the reason behind the huge outstanding accounts payable recorded as of year-end.  Procedures in terms of recording the account will be polished and the concerned departments will be properly instructed to prevent the accumulation of the payable account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Development Council (a) continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund; and (b) direct the City Engineering Office and City Planning and Development Officer to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the  early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Officer-In-Charge of the City Engineer’s Office (CEO) explained in her letter- reply dated March 8, 2017, that two   projects remained unimplemented because they were found practically not workable although these were included in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) of the City. The CEO is currently working to polish the procedures on project implementation starting from the identification of project priorities, preparation of   program of works, monitoring of actual implementation of the projects and   until the full accomplishment thereof.  Further, the Office had coordinated with the Management Information and Technology Division (MITD) for the adoption of the Geographic Information System (GIS) to facilitate the documentation of project implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to instruct the Accountant to send demand letters to officers and employees requiring settlement of their unliquidated cash advances.  Adhere to the provisions on the liquidation of cash advances as prescribed by Section 89 of PD 1445 and  Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Cashier to require the collectors to turn over collections daily.
Verify the turned over collections to ensure accuracy of the RCD.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to regularly conduct physical inventory of its properties and inventories every year to determine the existence and physical condition of the assets.
Provide a supporting schedule for each PPE and inventory account in the FS.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Accountant and the LDRRM focal person to adhere to the existing guidelines on the accounting and reporting of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF).  
Implement the PPAs reflected in the 70% Mitigating Funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the required schedule of submission of the monthly Financial Statements, together with the SAAOB, journals and the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2002-03 and COA Circular  No. 94-001, for timely verification of transactions and accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Local Development Council (LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the LDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP and the monthly utilization report for LDRRMF.
observations.reason: There was a confusion on who has to prepare the LDRRMFIP and the Utilization Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Management will create an Inventory Committee to be headed by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Though it has been a subject of repeated recommendations for several years, still there is no Inventory Committee that will facilitate the inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to prepare the updated bank reconciliation statements for all funds and submit the same to COA for verification. The same will be used as basis to ascertain and validate the cash in bank balances recorded in the Financial Statements for CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: Proper segregation of duties in the Accounting Department.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower in the accounting department was the reason in the non-preparation of the bank reconciliation statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the General Services Officer to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.reason: See related audit observation no. 12

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City Officials to:
Reconcile the records of PPE accounts of the Accounting Office, General Services Office and Assessor’s Office.
Transfer land properties in the name of the City Government and reclassify them according to their actual use.
Make the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the major repairs and maintenance, and derecognize the items that were replaced for fair presentation of the affected accounts in financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned officials to:
Exhaust all remedies to collect the receivables such as sending demand letters to last known addresses.
Exert efforts to locate the documents to support the claims and ascertain accuracy and fair presentation of the receivable accounts.
We further recommended that the City Government establish a sound internal control on the collection and recording of receivables.

We recommended that management consider the additional investment of idle funds in short-term time deposits or HYSA to generate additional revenue for the city. This shall be done with the authority of the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

We recommended that management charge the said expenditures under the General Fund instead of the 20% Development Fund as these did not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures although these were expended to support basic programs mandated to be implemented by the LGU. We further recommended that the management, in the ensuing years, to identify and implement programs and projects charged against the 20% Development Fund in accordance to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned officials to comply with the minimum standards set forth by the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) to strengthen a safer, adaptive and disaster-resilient Filipino community towards sustainable development.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
Sections 4.1.5 and 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 be adhered to in the grant of cash advance.
For the Officiating and Delegation Officials to submit Certification from their Agency Accountant that no traveling expenses (which includes meals and incidental expenses) were claimed by them and for the concerned officials to refund the amount totaling to ₱63,000.00.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that a system of check and balance and monitoring of leaves and absences be established by the City Government. Specifically, we recommended that:
The Department Heads ensure that all entries in the DTRs are filled-out (leaves, holidays, official travels/seminars must all be indicated) and for the Accounting Department to see to it the Application for Leave, Travel Orders and necessary supporting documents are attached to DTRs.
The HRMO to submit to this Office, certified photocopy of employees leave card as of December 31, 2017, prioritizing the employees mentioned in the sampling schedule. Further, we recommend that the HRMO certify balances of leave credits in the Application for Leave as of the month preceding the month when the employee applied for a leave.
The Local Chief Executive to grant monetization of leave credits judiciously especially that inconsistencies of balances of leave credits were observed. Further, we recommend for the City Administrator or his representative to review records and look into the reasons of inconsistent balances and that sanctions be imposed to those officials and employees responsible for the inconsistent balances.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
The LGU to be judicious in the implementation of its programs. The need for consultant in the conduct of activities be thoroughly reviewed in the interest of economy and efficiency.
For the consultant to refund the amount of ₱10,495.00 for the hotel accommodation and meals.
Provisions of the Revised IRR be strictly adhered to. Advance Payment be strictly not to exceed 15% of the contract amount and for the LGU to compel the posting of security.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-GSO to do the following:
Properly accomplish the Vehicle Trip Tickets for every use of the government vehicle to be attached for every gasoline requisition/purchase. The Trip Ticket should be prenumbered with the inclusion of certification from passenger that he used the vehicle for authorized purpose.
Be guided by the average minimum and maximum gasoline and oil consumption of the different types of motor vehicles in authorizing gasoline issuance.
Prepare and submit within the prescribed period the Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report to closely monitor and control the fuel consumption.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LGU establish specific guidelines in the grant of financial assistance in line with DSWD MC No. 02s. 2014. Specifically, we recommended that:
Financial assistance is to be given for public purposes only and not for funding of specific activities of private groups and individuals as provided in Sections 4 (2) of PD 1445 and 335 of RA 7160. Moreover, guidelines of COA Circular No. 2007-001 be followed in the release of funds to NGOs/POs.
Assistance in the form of goods, supplies or items shall not be granted through cash assistance to ensure compliance of rules and regulations of procurement law.
The City Government ensure actual utilization of assistance given and require submission of Fund Utilization Report especially to assistance made to government agencies. Likewise, assistance be not made to representatives of government offices and instead, procedures be followed in the liquidation or disbursement of government transactions.
Endorsements of the members SP need not be the basis in the grant of financial assistance to ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance among qualified beneficiaries.

We recommended for the Bamboo Processing Center to operate in full scale and the City Mayor direct the concerned officials to:
Rigorously train more members of the association on bamboo processing especially on those bamboo crafts for replication (Annex F-2).
Closely supervise the production, marketing and management of the Bamboo Processing Center to sustain the technology improvement facility for the bamboo industry.
Conduct a demonstration on charcoal-making in order to utilize the equipment and machineries procured for the purpose to capacitate the farmers and increase their income.
Review the proposal of the livelihood projects to ascertain that only the necessary equipment and machineries directly attributable to the long-term implementation of the project will be procured.
Create a committee to review the proposals of different associations for the livelihood projects funded by National Government Agencies so that holistic approach will be applied and increase the success in the implementation of the projects.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a) direct the City Accountant to: (i) record the land and  other properties that remained not recognized in the books; (ii) classify the PPE accounts to its proper category/group; (iii) use the EUL in the computation of depreciation expense as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2003-007; (iv) adopt the principles prescribed in PPSAS 17 for proper accounting of the City’s PPE; (b) require the official concerned to facilitate the transfer of title of all the City properties  to recognize the ownership thereof.; and (c) instruct the CGSO and CAO to effect the reconciliation of all PPE accounts.

We also reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) investigation of the properties not found during the physical count and take appropriate actions against accountable personnel/officials as warranted; and (b) Property Team to identify additional personnel to reinforce the inventory team to pave the way for the completion of the physical count of all properties for a more reliable information on PPE and fair presentation of the asset accounts in the financial statements at year-end.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require (a) the inventory team to (i) conduct physical count of all inventory items regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within the prescribed deadline; and (ii) reconcile the records of the CGSO with the Accounting records on a per category basis; and (b) all offices to request only for the required quantity of supplies to avoid obsolescence of goods, or loss due to misuse or possible theft.

We recommended that the City Accountant continue to: (a) review timely all outstanding payables to ensure that cancelled, invalid or waived obligations are removed from the account and reverted to the General Fund and which had been outstanding for more than two years and without actual claims, administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected contracts or records; and (b) maintain an aging of accounts payable to facilitate proper monitoring of the movement of the Accounts Payable.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the City Accountant follow the check disbursement process stipulated in Section 44 of the NGAS Manual and ensure that recognition of accounts payable is based on valid claims supported with complete documentation and approved DVs.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure the timely submission of cash advance vouchers for Confidential Fund along with the pertinent documents to enable the auditor for a timely review in the grant thereof as well as the immediate submission of liquidation documents to the ICFAU at the COA Central Office pursuant to Sections 6.1.8.4 and 6.2.2 of COA-DBM-DILG-GOCC-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Disposal Committee to proceed with the prompt disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with prescribed procedures; (b) direct the City General Services Officer to ensure that all government properties no longer serviceable are disposed of at the earliest opportunity to prevent further deterioration and reduction in their economic value.

We recommended that the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC); TWG and BAC Secretariat (i) abide by the latest/applicable rules and regulations of the DPWH in the evaluation of the correctness of the ABC for infrastructure projects; (ii) invite the appropriate observers at all stages of the procurement process and notify them of any change/s in schedule; (iii) improve compliance by the City in the posting of procurement activities within the required media and within the prescribed time;(b) General Services Officer (i) cause the contract to be signed within the required period of ten (10) calendar days from receipt of NOA by the contractor for all procurements;(c) City Accountant (i) see to it that progress payments on projects are subjected to retention; (d)City Engineer (i) require the contractors to address the issue on warranty security and issue an amended certificate of acceptance to save the City from possible unnecessary expenditures in the future; (ii) discontinue the practice of automatically issuing certificate of acceptance on projects after completion; (iii) require the contract awardee to post warranty security for all procurements; (iv) along with the BAC, TWG, BAC Secretariat, implementing or procuring officers, keep abreast with the latest guidelines on the procurement of goods, services and civil works and on their contract implementation to obtain reasonable assurance that the City obtained and implemented its contracts at the most advantageous terms and in a transparent and correct manner.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive: (a) require the CEO to complete the remaining projects in the AIP for CY 2017; (b) direct the City Engineering Office and City Planning and Development Officer to undertake the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period; and (c) require the Planning and Development Coordinator to coordinate with the CBO and CEO regarding the utilization of 20% DF and prepare accurate year-end report thereon showing actual completed projects.

We recommended that the Local School Board of City of San Fernando: (a) furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Teacher Deployment Analysis Report and Registry of Qualified Applicants (RQA) for CY 2017; and (b) utilize the Special Education Fund only for purposes explicitly allowed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GPBP focal person of the respective implementing offices to: (a) complete the remaining projects and strictly monitor the implementation of projects as targeted and planned; (b) take note of any gap for future inputs especially on the delay of implementation and completion of GPBP-funded projects and programs; and (c) submit and coordinate with the City Accounting Office regarding the timely liquidation of project disbursements to their source agencies.

We recommended that Management: (a) in coordination with the CSWD and OSCA consider proposing other beneficial programs for SCs and PWDs aside from the conduct of General Assemblies; (b) consider the procurement of meals through direct payment instead of coursing it through a cash advance; (c) conduct measures to give priority to only those who are indigent and qualified are included in the City’s database; and (d) conduct sufficient information dissemination regarding programs in order to increase the number of attendees in the City’s programs.

We commended the Management on its continuous efforts in addressing disaster risk reduction and management and climate change issues by substantially adhering to the provisions of RA 10121, DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-079 and NDRRMC Memorandum Circular No. 4, s. 2012. On the other hand, we recommended that the Management (a) prioritize the establishment of permanent evacuation centers and warehouse dedicated only for DRRM use to be fully disaster-ready at all times; and (b) comply with the essential facilities listed in the provisions of Unnumbered Joint Memorandum Circular of DILG, DSWD, DOH and DepED series of 2013 entitled “Guidelines on Evacuation Center Coordination and Management”   in the establishment of evacuation centers.
We recommended that Management settle the audit suspensions within the prescribed period and continue to review claims properly for regularity to prevent audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Management (a) perform a thorough planning and due diligence in the procurement of transport and heavy equipment to ensure maximum utilization of public resources; (b) as far as financial resources allow, refrain from procuring second-hand/reconditioned vehicles to avoid costly repairs and maintenance on reconditioned equipment in the long run; and (c) strictly adhere to DBM NBC 446-A in the procurement of reconditioned motor vehicles in cases when acquisition of brand new units by outright purchase is not feasible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the City Administrator require the overall Project Supervisor to: (a) enjoin department heads to cooperate and commit themselves to pave the way for the completion of the project; (b) ensure that projects are properly monitored and assessed to prevent further delays in implementations;  (c) ensure that parallel run is implemented to ensure accuracy of generated reports; and (d) impose liquidated damages to the service provider for the long delay of the contract.

We recommended that management: (a) continue to develop the 5-year BP and submit it to the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to pave the way for its legal creation as an economic enterprise to enable it to increase its income and sustain its operations in the long-run; (b) consider the imposition of reasonable fees to students to expand its income and improve its sustainability status; (c) maximize the use of facilities and classrooms thru marketing of additional students; (d) consider prioritizing qualified enrollees in the City College under the City’s Scholarship Program to facilitate flow of income within the City.

We recommended that: (a) Local Finance Committee and City Legal Officer in partnership with PADC (i) initiate the provision of an escalation clause in favor of the City on its share of its income in the JV, if feasible  regardless of increase in the users’ fees in the slaughterhouse; (ii) initiate the study on the possible increase of fees collected for slaughterhouse operations or automatically indexing user fees to inflation rates; (b) City Accountant (i) prepare adjusting entry for the misstatement noted under the Share in Profit of Joint Venture; (ii) maintain special accounts for the City Slaughterhouse in the GF (iii) account for jointly-controlled assets in the books (c) Management (i) coordinate with the consultant for the BCS regarding the error/misposting in account; (ii) conduct regular assessment and monitoring of the slaughterhouse’ performance and create necessary measures to improve its performance (d) City Accountant in coordination with PADC prepare financial reports such as: (i) Statement of Income and Expenses; (ii) Cash Flow Statement; (iii) Schedule of Accounts Payable; (iv) Schedule of Accounts Receivable; and (v) other related financial reports; (e) LFC prepare the SAFSSR and FSSE report to monitor performance of the slaughterhouse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections as mandated by Section 307 of R.A.                   No. 7160 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. Moreover, require the Barangay Treasurer to prepare at the close of each month RAAF and CRAAF and submit the same on or before the 5th day of the following month in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate/settle their long outstanding cash advances and instruct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the internal control on the grant and liquidation of cash advances by complying with the provisions under Section Nos. 4.1.2 and 5.1.3 of the same COA Circular. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the accountable officers to deposit immediately the undeposited collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and to initiate corrections/adjustments in order to come up with all accounts in their normal balances fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a)	the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of the physical count as reported in the RPCPPE and RPCI with the accounting records; and
the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method in the recording of purchases instead of recording them directly to expense accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor who also heads the Municipal Solid Waste Board to follow up the comment and/or approval of the NSWMC to the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan transmitted by the municipality. Moreover, ensure the development of Sanitary Landfill as final disposal site for solid and residual wastes of the municipality in compliance with Section Nos. 17 and 37 of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the BAC to submit the Statement of Time Elapsed, Minutes of Bid Opening, Post Qualification Reports and Notice of Post Qualifications and adhere strictly to the provisions of the                          2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement process; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims for payment of the above infrastructure projects in compliance with Section 4.6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking documents and make sure that the disbursements charged against the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund are supported with complete documentation as required by COA Circular                        No. 2012-001;
instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer to Special Trust Fund the unspent balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2017 in compliance with Items 4.4  and 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of previous years' unexpended balance of LDRRMF in compliance with Item 5.1.16 and Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of identified developmental PPAs and maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund; and
direct the MPDC to ensure compliance with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to fast track the implementation of the unexpended/unobligated DRRM programs/activities/projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure that  municipal  lots
are registered  under  the  Torrens  Title  System  to  better  protect  the
interest of the municipal  government.  Proper  reconciliation  between  the
records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s  Office  should  also
be done.  We also recommend that the  Assessor’s  Office  monitor  regularly
the status and the purpose of these  lots.   Additional  income  could  have
been earned by the Municipality had these lots been utilized efficiently  as
sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.

We recommend that the BAC and Punong Barangay cause the  submission  of
the lacking documents and submit a valid explanation in regard  to  the
deficiencies observed  in  the  other  supporting  documents  to  avoid
disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipality pro-actively make  follow  up  with
the LBP and not just wait for the bank’s instructions to hasten the  payment
of payroll through the use of the ATMs  to  promote  efficiency  and  proper
accountability.

We recommend for the immediate submission of the  printed  notices  of
public bidding culled from the PhilGEPS website  to  prove  compliance  with
the Procurement Law aimed at transparency and competition in order  for  the
Municipality to obtain the above goods at prices most advantageous to it  to
prevent possible disallowance of the transaction.


      We also recommend that  municipal  officials  refrain  from  approving
disbursements unless all the documentary requirements are  attached  to  the
claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We recommend that the BAC Officials fill out the required  information
in the PhilGEPS website in order for interested stakeholders  to  understand
the  whole  procurement  process.  Moreover,  such  should  be  updated   in
accordance with the stage in the procurement  process,  so  that  Notice  of
Award and Notice to Proceed  can  be  posted  and  updated  accordingly  for
transparency. Furthermore, we recommend  that  municipal  officials  refrain
from approving disbursements unless all  the  documentary  requirements  are
attached to the claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan Secretary submit  the  Minutes
of the regular  sessions/attendance  sheets/actual  work/attendance  of  the
official vis-à-vis the work schedule or performance  standards  duly  signed
by the   SB Members/Municipals Officials/Resource Persons who  were  present
during the Regular/Special Sessions   Furthermore,  we  recommend  that  the
Municipal Treasurer see to  it  that  all  disbursement  vouchers  are  duly
supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

We recommend strict compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section
5.3 of the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2014-1  in  the
staffing of the Municipality’s LDRRM Office for  proper  accountability  and
in order  for  it  to  effectively  fulfill  and  achieve  its  mandate  and
objectives.

We recommend that the Municipality appropriate in their Annual  Budget
one  percent  (1%)  of  its  IRA  for  the  implementation/strengthening  of
programs, projects and activities of their respective LCP.  Such  allocation
should be duly supported with specific, practical and doable plans.  Failure
to comply is punishable by a fine of not less  than  Twenty  Thousand  Pesos
(P20,000.00) but not more than Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00)  or  suffer
imprisonment of not less than eight (8) years but not  more  than  ten  (10)
years, or both, such fine and imprisonment at the discretion of  the  court,
unless a higher penalty is  provided  for  in  the  Revised  Penal  Code  or
special laws. If the offender  is  a  public  officer  or  employee,  he/she
shall,  in  addition   to   such   fine   and/or   imprisonment,   be   held
administratively liable and shall suffer the penalty of  perpetual  absolute
disqualification.

We  recommend  that  the  Accounting  Section  strictly  enforce   the
provisions of Section 47 of  the  General  Appropriations  Act  of  2017  on
statutory net pay to aid in the promotion  of  an  efficient  and  effective
public service by ensuring that personnel will receive  adequate  amount  of
money for their daily subsistence.

We recommended the management to instruct the Accounting to exert more efforts in the retrieval of prior years’ records to identify the accountable officers/employees/debtors or concerned agencies liable for the unaccounted balance of P1,106,438.33 and, if appropriate, file request for authority to write-off for claims which could no longer be accounted. We also recommended to require the Accounting to send demand letters to the identified accountable officer/employee or recipient agency of other receivables.

We recommended to management the following courses of action:
Require the inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking in accordance with the guidelines provided in Handbook on Property & Supply Management System and invite the COA Auditor to witness the same.
Submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year.
Require the Accounting in-charge to provide a supporting schedules for each PPE and inventory account in the FS.

We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the required schedule of submission of the monthly Financial Statements, together with the SAAOB, journals and the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents as required in Section 347 of RA No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006, for timely verification of transactions and accounts.

We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payroll and collection reports in consistent with the existing rules and regulations and in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Accounting Unit to provide correct cash balances by reconciling noted negative balances and proper monitoring of the subsidiary ledgers. Submit detailed explanation to the Audit Team for the negative balances.
We also recommended to request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the 20% development fund.

We recommended that the management require the Accountable Officer (AO) to make a written explanation on the noted deficiency. We recommended further that the Municipal Treasurer together with the liquidating officer closely monitor the daily remittance of the collections of the AO and ensure that the  internal control policies concerning the withdrawals of accountable forms - by the AOs (with unremitted balances) should be strictly followed.

Require the:
Human Resource Management Officer to issue a memorandum to require the concerned employees to sign their DTRs that are on-file at the HRMO and create a policy to avoid the lapses on the accomplishment and submission of DTRs; and
Accountant to submit all the paid payrolls and their supporting documents, including the months of October 2017 through December 2017 and ensure that all claims against the government are properly supported with all the documentary requirements.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer to strictly adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing and granting of applications for terminal leave benefits by its officials and employees.

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9344 and appropriate at least one percent (1%) of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of PPAs of the LCPC and utilize the said allocation pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.
We recommended to management to instruct the LDRRM focal person to adhere to the existing guidelines on the accounting and reporting of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF).

We recommended that before joining trainings and seminars organized by a private training institution, the Management should verify first if the organizing body is an accredited institution of the Civil Service Commission or Government Procurement Policy Board in case of the seminars involving RA 9184 to ensure that the agency will be spending government funds to trainings and seminars that meet Qualification Standards.

We recommended that the management abide with the guidelines on procurement provided by RA 9184 to avoid the possible audit disallowances and to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement process.

Require the recipients refund the value of the mobile phones; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify if there is a legal basis for a particular transaction before signing the disbursement vouchers for payments and assure that supporting documents are completely attached.

We recommended to management to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures and to strictly adhere to the disclosure requirements of PPSAS in the Notes to Financial Statements and ensure that the financial reports can be easily understood by the intended users.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned office and officials to ensure the full implementation of the projects funded by the 20% development fund to speed up development and progress and eventually to attain the desired outcomes of the projects and to enable the constituents to enjoy their full benefits.
We further recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to demand for liquidation of previous fund transfer from the concerned barangay officials.
We also recommended to require the Local Development Committee to prioritize the development projects that are workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and completion, thus attaining the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in an efficient and effective manner.
