observations.reason: The GAD Focal person is not knowledgeably performing its proper submission of the GPB to the PPDO and in the DILG Regional Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality enact a Municipal Ordinance establishing the Solid Waste Management Board, prepare Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality, implement no segregation, no collection policy, and stop the practice of open burning of garbage.
observations.managementAction: The Management promised to comply with the above mentioned Republic Act by creating Solid Waste Management Board and preparing Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 1. Processing of disbursements should only be undertaken to those which were properly approved/signed and with complete supporting documentation; 2. The Municipal Treasurer submit to the Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and 3. The Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to timely submit the disbursement vouchers forwarding the disbursement and collection accounts to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: There is a ysyal delay in the office of the Municipal Treasurer in forwarding the Disbursement and Collection Accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council (LDC) to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We further recommended that the Agency follow the proper way of allocating budget for LDRRMF. The total appropriations for LDRRMF should at least be 5% of total estimated/budgeted revenue sources of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRC relied only on the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Plan, which was intended for the Agency’s Plan for 2014-2018 in terms of Disaster Preparedness. They believed the preparation of this would suffice.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning and Development Officer provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year to monitor the status of implementation of each infrastructure project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Engineer to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year, list of all on-going programs/projects/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer was not aware and informed with regard to the deadline of such needed report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Revenue Collection Officers to strictly turnover all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the Municipal Treasurer as required under Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for the Local Government Units. Likewise, the collections turned over to the Municipal Treasurer including his collections has to be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed that this time, remittances of the accountable officers of collections will be more strict, by adhering to the policy that all collections be deposited intact and in full amount at the end of the business day. She also assured that cashbooks will be updated so as to have a proper monitoring of their monthly cash accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to prepare the updated bank reconciliation statements for all funds and submit the same to COA for verification. The same will be used as basis to ascertain and validate the cash in bank balances recorded in the Financial Statements for CY 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to prepare the updated Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and to submit the same on or before the 15th day of the following month.
observations.reason: The delayed receipt of bank statements from its depository banks and the lack of manpower in the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the City Accountant make necessary
adjustment by recording the land account at acquisition cost and to reconcile
PPE accounts with the records of the General Services Officer. Likewise, for
the City Assessor to reclassify the Land account to conform to its actual use.

We have recommended that City Officials:
1. Intensify the collection of long outstanding Receivables.
2. Exert effort to locate the following:
 Documents to establish the balance of Due from National
Government Agencies account.
 Documents to support the negative balances in Other Receivables
account in order to ascertain the accuracy of the receivable
accounts.
 Details of the “unreconciled portion” in the Other Receivables
account and list of LSB teachers with their corresponding
overpayment of salaries in order to facilitate collection.
3. Establish a sound internal control on the collection receivables.

We have recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the
reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to
ensure the accuracy of the accounts to ascertain their reliability.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the
following:
1. All payments be made by check, for effective internal control and to
be able to pinpoint responsibility unless it would be impractical to do
so.
2. The practice of transferring cash advances from one Accountable
Officer to another be stopped in conformity to the provisions of
Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular 97-002.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the
following:
 For the CSWDO to require the preparation and submission of the
Fund Utilization Reports of the four (4) associations pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 for them to conduct periodic
inspection and monitoring of the livelihood projects to ascertain
economy, efficiency and effectiveness of operations.
 Require the associations to record their transactions in a cashbook
and for the disbursements to be authorized and supported with
official receipts.
 Conduct assessment/feasibility study of livelihood project proposals in
the future to ascertain their sustainability.
 Require the President of Masaganang Samahan ng Ilocanos Sur to
immediately deposit the amount of P143,447.00 representing
withdrawals made which were not accounted for.
 Require the President and Treasurer of Saoay Hog Raisers SLPA to
submit all documents pertaining to the withdrawals, disbursements
and record of sales of the hog raising project for audit.

In view of this, we have recommended that the City Officials review
the Approved 2016 New Staffing Pattern of the City Government to ensure
compliance with Section 13 (c) of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 19 and
ascertain that all positions created are necessary and needed. In the interest
of efficiency and economy, the functions of any office may be consolidated
with those of another.

We have recommended that the LGU create an Inspectorate Team or
Project Monitoring Committee to facilitate extensive monitoring and
evaluation of implemented projects having the City Engineer as mandatory
member inorder to establish a sound internal control in the implementation
of projects. Inspection Reports resulting hereto be used as basis for the
issuance of Certificate of Completion. Likewise, we recommended that close
monitoring of projects be undertaken during its construction to ensure
compliance with plans and specifications.

We have recommended the following:
 Only the actual fare for travel be charged against the SEF.
 Expenses charged against SEF conform to Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and
Paragraph 4.0 of conform DECS (now DepEd), DBM and DILG Joint
Circular No. 01 s. 1998.
 Liquidation reports be submitted by recipients of cash assistance to be
attached as supporting documents to disbursement voucher.
 Procurement of goods and services comply with the provisions of RA
9184.

We have recommended that policies and procedures in procurement
under alternative mode be strictly adhered to. We likewise recommended
that Purchase Orders be properly and duly accomplished and require
suppliers to strictly adhere to terms and conditions of procured items.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring entity direct
the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Project Monitoring Report
approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the management propose/implement
projects in an efficient and effective manner pursuant to Section 2 of PD
1445 to avoid loss or wastage of government funds and property.

We have recommended the following:
I. For both BHCs
1. Availability of drugs and medicines at all times
a. Period of procurement be observed so that target implementation
dates are followed as scheduled
b. Family planning supplies and other medicines be not DOH
dependent and be considered and included in the City’s APP
2. Provision of above-mentioned supplies and equipment
3. Form requirements be adhered to
4. DOH-mandated courses be attended by personnel
5. Information dissemination especially to least known programs be
conducted
6. Support to services under cancer, STD/HIV and tuberculosis control
program be established, at least on preventive manner.
II. For Poro BHC - Provision of additional ventilation equipment
III. For Bangbangolan BHC
 Consider construction of stairs and/or installation of handrails to
Bangbangolan BHC
 Properly accomplish Monthly Drugs Inventory Report
A separate Citizen Participatory Audit (CPA) Report was made and
transmitted to the Office of the City Mayor incorporating therein the overall
observations in the conduct of CPA of Bangbangolan and Poro BHCs.

We recommended that the LDRRMC and CPDO (a) continue their concerted efforts in providing valuable programs and projects through various development plans aligned with the regional plans; and (b) ensure that the yearly updates of the respective plans are committed as well, whenever necessary.

We recommended that the (a) CTO and the support offices continue their commitment in complying with existing rules and regulations related to disaster preparedness particularly on the receipt and utilization of in-kind donations; and (b) the General Service Officer and the City Treasurer make the proper communication with the donor on the appropriate action for the spoiled items.

We recommended that the City Accountant and the concerned offices continue to comply with the appropriate documentation of disbursements relative to the PCL seminars and those pertinent to the Leagues of Cities, Municipalities, Barangays and others.

We recommended that the Accounting Office: (a) continue to abide with the provisions of the GSIS Act of 1997 as regards the remittances of applicable contributions and premiums as well as government share within the prescribed period; and (b) review the prior years’  transactions to eliminate the discrepancy of ₱114,634.60 in the books. The Accounting Office may check with the records whether all Personal Share (PS) deducted from the payroll of employees and the Government Share (GS) and Employee’s Compensation Contribution (ECC) for all employees in previous years were all remitted and cause the immediate remittance to GSIS for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees.

We recommended that the City Accountant and her staff (a) continue being compliant with the revenue regulations on the withholding of taxes from compensation of employees and creditable taxes for payments to suppliers and contractors and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period; and (b) exert immediate efforts to resolve the cause of the discrepancies in the Due to BIR – General Fund account.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) investigation of the properties not accounted for during the physical count and take appropriate action against accountable personnel/officials; and (b) completion of the physical count of all assets under PPE and related accounts for improved accountability over the assets and accuracy of the year-end reported balances of the PPE accounts.
We also recommended that the LCE (a) provide the needed additional personnel to the property team to ensure that property records are complete and updated and conduct of the physical count is accomplished within the prescribed period; and (b) direct the City Accountant to (i) ensure that all government property officially received by the City are accounted for and recognized in the books at acquisition cost or at appraised value, as applicable; (ii) maintain adequate property records for PPE so that relevant details are available to users thereof; and (iii) adopt all the principles prescribed in PPSAS 17 for proper accounting of the City’s PPE.

We recommended that the City Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry to transfer the balance from the Cash-Local Treasury account to the Due from Officers and Employees account to fairly present balances of affected accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer (a) exercise prudence and due diligence in the processing of transactions; (b) require the disbursing officer to liquidate the cash advances on time; (c) ensure that no cash advances are granted unless the previous ones are liquidated; (d) refer to the revised chart of accounts for guidance to avoid misclassification of accounts; and (e) see to it that all applicable laws, rules and regulations affecting cash advances and disbursement of funds are strictly adhered to.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Treasurer (a) continue the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers as basis for the setting up of receivables; (b) update the posting of real property tax payments in the RPTAR; (c) monitor the completion of the enhanced information system in order to generate more accurate and timely reports.

We recommended that the (a) City Administrator/Local Chief Executive (i) render written explanation on the absence of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the City and its component barangays for the subject financial assistance transactions; (ii) consider granting the financial assistance to barangays, especially those lacking in technical competencies, in completed project form instead of in cash thus making the City as the implementer and the barangay as the beneficiary; and iii) ensure that a MOA between the City and its barangays are executed before a transfer of funds/projects is facilitated; and (b) Accounting Office (i) require the implementing heads of barangay to: (i.a) exhaust all efforts to coordinate with the previous Barangay Administration to facilitate the submission of liquidation documents for the long overdue unliquidated financial assistance;  (i.b) religiously submit all liquidation documents to the City Accounting Office for all financial assistance received;  (i.c) immediately refund the unutilized balance of the financial assistance to the City Treasurer’s Office after the purpose of the grant is completed; and (i.d) strictly observe the proper procedures in the liquidation of financial assistance and ensure completeness of documents upon submission to the accounting personnel in-charge of the books of the barangay;( ii) ensure that all required liquidation documents for previously granted financial assistance have been submitted and issue a Certification that funds previously transferred to the Implementing Agency (IA) have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before processing the grant/transfer of another financial assistance; (iii) closely monitor the compliance of the recipient barangays on the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period; and (iv) draw journal entries to recognize all settlements already complied with by the barangays concerned relative to the prior years’ financial assistance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the (a) Inventory Team (i) conduct physical count of all inventory items regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within the prescribed deadline; and (ii) reconcile the records of the CGSO with the Accounting  records on a per category basis; (b) the CGSO and Accounting Office  (i) ensure the conduct of regular reconciliation of subsidiary records on a quarterly basis and (ii) address the issues surrounding the failure to reconcile the records between  the Accounting Office and the CGSO; and (c) Department Heads of all offices request only the necessary supplies to avoid over-stocking and to avoid accumulation of various supplies at the CGSO’s CSA and prevent spoilage of goods, or loss due to misuse or possible theft.
We further recommended that the (a) CGSO consolidate weekly the RIS using the SSMI and submit to the Accounting office for recording of issued supplies; and (b) the Accounting office follow the prescribed point in the recognition of supplies purchases or inventory in the books.

We recommended that the City Accountant (a) review or analyze all outstanding payables to pave the way for the (i) de-recognition in the books of all obligations of the City that had already been extinguished thru payment, cancellation or other means; and (ii)reversion  to the General Fund those which had been outstanding for more than two years and without actual claims, administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected contracts or records; (b) follow the check disbursement process described in Section 44 of the NGAS Manual and ensure that recognition of accounts payable is based on valid claims supported with adequate documentation and approved DVs;(c) maintain an aging of accounts payable to facilitate proper monitoring of the movement of the Accounts Payable; and (d)post entries on a per transaction basis in the  subsidiary ledger to allow for matching of payment with the recorded payables and timely de-recognition or adjustment of excess amount of payables arising from premature recognition of contract amounts as payables.

We recommended that the City Accountant submit to the Audit Team without further delay the cash advance vouchers together with the  pertinent documents required under  COA-DBM-DILG-GOCC-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 for post audit.

We recommended that the:(a) City Accountant and City Treasurer: (i) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports so as not to delay the verification of transactions and post-audit activities; and(ii) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team;(b) City Accountant require the accounting staff concerned to scan only documents which did not undergo scanning or reproduction from preceding or originating departments; and (c) Local Chief Executive: (i) require all concerned accountable officers to submit on time all financial documents and reports for their respective transactions for each month for the preparation of the financial reports; and(ii) impose sanctions against erring accountable officers who continuously fail to abide with the timely submission of documents and reports.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel concerned to comply with dispatch the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for CY 2015 AAR and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Development Council (a) continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund; and (b) direct the City Engineering Office and City Planning and Development Officer to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the  early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the: (a) LSB(i) carefully study, plan and determine the annual budgetary needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the City which shall be supported by programs, projects and activities under Section 100 of R.A. No. 7160 as updated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2017-001; and(ii) members of the LSB maximize the utilization of SEF within the ambit of its intended objectives; and(b) City Budget Officer and City Accountant monitor the proper utilization and charging of expenditures to SEF.

We recommended that the CDRRMO and the LDRRMC take the necessary action for the timely and proper recommendation to the SP for the lifting of the state of calamity under appropriate conditions thereafter, in order to prevent any untoward abuse of the circumstances.

We recommended that the CSWDO as the lead office in the disaster preparedness aspect of determining the adequate and appropriate relief goods both for stockpiling and distribution to disaster victims (a)  summarize in database form immediately as possible all information in the access cards; and (b) conduct actual validation of the affected families and individuals sheltered in the designated evacuation camps as well as those in the community on a per barangay basis,  to include  data such as age group, assessed needs, and health condition for those with maintenance medication, in order to come up with the updated disaster victims’ profile to ensure adequacy of the volume of relief goods for stockpiling and distribution and to serve as basis for the ensuing year’s procurement planning.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) CSWDO  to (i) closely monitor camp situations in order to provide reasonable comfort to disaster victims through appropriate services, goods, and facilities giving due importance to gender concerns; and (ii) ensure that the planning of procurement needs is made in the first quarter of the year, ahead of the anticipated disaster to address the basic needs for food, clothing, medicines, and shelter and procurement inputs are communicated to concerned officials in a timely manner (iii) submit the validated list/profiles of affected families and individuals per age group to the head of the GSO to come up with a realistic number of disaster victims in order to procure the necessary and adequate basic needs of victims during disaster, taking into consideration the latest data on the recent calamity; (iv) prepare a procurement list of relief goods to include food supplies for children and newborns, if warranted, based on validated data; (b) CDRRMO to (i) ensure the availability and serviceable condition of all rescue equipment and vehicles for efficient and effective disaster response; (d) City Health Officer to submit to the CDRRMO the procurement request, ideally in the 1st quarter of the year for the necessary medicines and medical supplies for water and air-borne diseases for consolidation of procurement for disaster preparedness; and (e) City Engineer to (i) prepare a program of work for the tail dike project for submission to the National and Provincial Governments for possible funding allocation; (ii) include in its budget proposal the allocation for evacuation center equipped with adequate facilities for senior citizens, special children, newborn, and with consideration to gender concerns.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and Technical Working Group: (a) prepare the GAD database in coordination with the LPDO which will serve as basis for planning, implementing, and formulating GAD-responsive activities; (b) use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in monitoring and evaluating GAD activities; (c) submit to the Audit Team the annual accomplishment report for evaluation; and (d)  improve further the schedule in GAD planning and budgeting as prescribed under Joint Circular 2013-01 of DILG, PCW, DBM, and NEDA.

We recommended that the: (a) Local Health Board and/or the City Health Office prepare a PhilHealth Per Family Payment Rate Fund Utilization and Program Plan that is integrated in the City’s Annual Investment Plan; (b) Accounting Office comply with the submission of the required PFP Disposition and Allocation Form every quarter and other reports needed for monitoring and evaluation of the program relative to the releases of the PFPR Fund as required by PhilHealth Circular No. 015, s. 2014; and(c) City Health Office and Accounting Office abide by the provisions of PhilHealth Circular No. 10, s. 2012 on the proper disposition and allocation of the Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: (a) CAVO to(i) closely monitor the deliveries of livestock for an efficient dispersal program in the future; and (ii) perform a regular visit to dispersed animals to determine their actual condition and prevent further undue losses;  and (b) GPBP focal person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the implementation of projects as targeted and planned and take note of any gap for future inputs especially on the delay of implementation and completion of GPBP-funded projects and programs.

We recommended that the:(a) City Traffic Management Division continue to implement the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-001, adopted thru City Ordinance No. 2008-015, and consider strengthening its information dissemination initiatives by posting more specific notices along the City boundaries on the  banning of tricycle operation in national highways;(b) Management (i) expedite the transfer of the CCTV control unit to facilitate a better monitoring operation not only by the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office but also by the City Traffic Management Division and Bureau of Fire Protection; and (ii) prioritize the need to upgrade the entire communication platform from wireless communication to a more reliable upgraded technology that will serve the recording and monitoring purpose of CCTV units.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board: (a) intensify the campaign to enjoin the majority of households’ participation on waste segregation, collection and disposal especially at the barangay level; (b) in coordination with the barangay officials and other offices/organizations, conduct information and education campaigns on the hazards of open burning, open dumpsites and other ill practices and enforce strictly the sanctions against violators; (c) ensure that the piles of garbage  of all barangays are properly collected  to prevent the residents from improperly disposing of their waste along the roads and other public places and from resorting to open burning of waste; (d) include in the Annual Investment Program PPAs that would ensure long-term management solid waste and implement the same; and (e) strengthen the information campaign by putting up signage in public places the acts prohibited under Section 48 of R.A. No. 9003 and the corresponding sanctions thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the City Administrator (a) require the overall Project Supervisor to (i)  submit written explanation for the lack of intervention to the delay in the delivery of systems to the City; (ii) submit an assessment report on the project’s actual status including issues surrounding the Project; (b) demand from the system developer the submission of a revised workplan  providing the timelines for the immediate completion of the required deliverables; (c) direct the City Accountant to hold the processing of claims of the system developer until pending issues are resolved in the City’s favor; (d) impose liquidated damages for the implementation delays of the system developer, as warranted; (d) ensure that projects are properly monitored and assessed to prevent further delays in implementations; (e) submit written explanation on the full payment to the system developer despite non-completion of the deliverables in the contract; (f) ensure that pilot testing is done, if parallel run is too costly,  during implementation of the system to mitigate the risk of data loss in case the system proved to be unsuccessful; (g) conduct change readiness briefing to departments concerned whenever migrating to an entirely new system to gain their support; (h) enjoin department heads to cooperate and commit themselves to pave the way for the completion of the project before the general revision of assessed values in CY 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in depositing the collections as mandated by Section 307 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
demand the immediate remittance and deposit of the unremitted collections totaling P672,755.08;
require the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Collecting Officers remit promptly and intact their collections up to the last OR issued, in compliance with Sections 29 and 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I; and
reclassify the amount of P672,755.08 from Advances to Officers and Employees to Due from Officers and Employees account for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make sure the preparation and submission by the Municipal Accountant of the monthly BRS for the previous years and for CY 2016 for all accounts maintained by the municipality with the Land Bank of the Philippines and Philippine National Bank pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce the immediate liquidation/settlement of long outstanding cash advances and instruct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the internal control on the grant and liquidation of cash advances by complying with the provisions under Section Nos. 4.1.2 and 5.1.3 of the same COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the registration and transfer of title of the land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality of San Fernando in compliance with Section 39 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Supreme Court Decision GR No. 163551.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee, not only the Property Officer-Designee, to conduct physical count semestrally for Inventories and annually for PPE, reconcile the results with the records of the Municipal Accountant and adjust the discrepancies noted, and submit the duly certified and approved RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and to initiate corrections/ adjustments to come up with correct account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the BAC to submit within the prescribed period all future contracts and other documents pertinent to all stages of the procurement process that form part of the contract in compliance with Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims for payment in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of the accounts Buildings and Construction in Progress – Buildings and Other Structures in compliance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to submit all monthly and quarterly financial and accounting reports and their corresponding statements within the prescribed period in order for the Audit Team to undertake timely review and post-audit of the transactions and accounts and at the same time to
We recommend Management to prioritize the implementation  of projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund so  that  expected  benefits  due  to intended recipients be timely achieved or delivered.

We recommend Management to properly use the BUB funds and  immediately implement the identified livelihood projects so that expected  benefits due to intended beneficiaries/recipients be delivered or achieved.
We recommend strict compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular  No. 17, series of 2015 on the implementation of the  Municipality’s  Social  Pension for Indigent Senior Citizens.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Accountant to send demand letters to officers and employees requiring settlement of  their unliquidated cash advances.  Adhere to the provisions on the liquidation of cash advances  as prescribed by Section 89 of PD 1445 and  Circular No. 97-002.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to instruct the Accounting unit to prepare and submit subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules to support the mentioned accounts, and provide the details on supporting schedule for the account  Other Receivables to ascertain the validity  and propriety of the accounts.    Verify the  composition of the account and send  letters to concerned debtors demanding payment due from them.
Finally, request for write off of accounts of those without details or information.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Accountant to set up the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable, based on taxpayer’s index card at the beginning of the year as required under Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Cashier to require the collectors to turn over collections daily.
Verify the turned over collections to ensure accuracy of the RCD.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to regularly conduct physical inventory of its properties and inventories every year to determine the existence and physical condition of the assets.
Provide a supporting schedule for each PPE and inventory account in the FS.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to  instruct the Accountant  and the LDRRM focal person to adhere to the existing guidelines on the accounting and  reporting of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF).
Implement the PPAs reflected in the 70% Mitigating Funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the required schedule of submission of the monthly Financial Statements, together with the SAAOB, journals and the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2002-03 and COA Circular  No. 94-001, for timely verification of transactions and accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, journal entry vouchers, payroll and collection reports in consistent with the existing rules and regulations and in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit the lacking proper supporting documents for the request for relief from accountability for the death of the carabao.
Ask for the duly signed and notarized copy of the Memorandum of Agreement relative to the program.
Ensure that the dispersed carabaos under the DA program are properly insured in compliance with the DA guideline.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the  Local Chief Executive to  require the concerned office and officials to see to it that projects under the 20% development fund are implemented as planned, in order to attain the desired outcomes of the projects and to enable the constituents to enjoy their full benefits.
Require liquidation of previous fund transfer from concerned  barangay officials.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management to avoid the conduct of out of town meetings to avoid incurrence of additional expenses by the local government.
In granting travelling expenses to LGU representatives, observe the provisions of EO 298.

We recommended that Management direct the property custodian to:

(a.) maintain individual subsidiary ledgers for each PPE account to facilitate
periodic reconciliation with the accounting records and proper
meonitoring thereof;

(b.) conduct reconciliation to reclassify the assets to its proper account and
eliminate the negative balances.

(c.) issue Acknowledgement Receipts of Equipment (ARE) (renewable every
three years) on all properties distributed and assign property numbers
for easy verification;

(d.) endeavor to conduct and prepare a complete physical count of all
properties of the municipality, and

(e.) prepare necessary report to perform proper disposal of unserviceable
properties.

We recommended that Management direct the immediate reconciliation of
the corresponding cash balances maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness and reliability of the reported cash
balances as of December 31, 2016,

We recommended that the Management require:

(a.) the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant, based
on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ index card, a duly
certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and
eollectible for the year; and

(b.) the Accountant that upon receipt of the certified list from the Municipal
Treasurer, record the expected revenue to be realized from the Real
Property Tax and Special Education Tax at the beginning of each year.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer:

a) to evaluate the status of the outsitanding checks of the LGU to determine
whether they are still in the custody of the cashier or in the hands of the
payees;

b) if checks were already issued, determine whether they are subject for
replacement or not;

¢) to effect/record in the books the necessary adjustments, as provided for
under Section 59 of the NGAs to ensure accuracy of the cash balance
appearing in the LGU’s financial reports;

d) that checks, when not claimed remain in the custody of the Municipal
Treasurer and be detached from the original voucher, to facilitate
determination of unclaimed vouchers at year-end;

e) to file photocopies of the vouchers with the individual check for easy
reference; and

f) to verify the state of additional stale checks for the year to determine the
correctness of its set-up in books and to make necessary adjustments
correspondingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the payables
arising from inter-agency transactions booked under the Due to LGUs account to its
proper liability accounts.

We recommended that Management:

(a) remind the officials and employees that not all letters of invitation to
seminars, trainings, conventions, etc. are mandatory, hence, give importance
to those that are useful and significant in relation to the affairs of the LGU.

(b) limit the number of participants to the attend the same seminar, trainings
and conventions which are held out of town/region. For conventions, priority
should be given to awardees and officers.

(c) take into consider‘:ztion the Yimited resources available and focus on more
important programs of the Municipality.

‘We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

(a.) direct the Municipal Health Office thru its concerned officers, to
immediately remit the total coliections from the Municipai Lying Clinic to
the Municipal Treasurer for proper issuance of official receipts and
recording;

(b.) instruct the concerned officers of the Municipal Health Office to
maintain records of services rendered by the Municipal Hospital, thru a
logbook or a registry, to be reconciled on a regular basis, with the fees
collected by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer;

(c.) direct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit records and reports
of collections to the Municipal Accountant for proper recoguition of income;
and
(d.) issue an order, to be disseminated orally and in writing to the
constituents of the LG, that any fees and charges imposed by the LGU, be
directly paid and remitted to the Municipal Treasurer for proper issuance of
an official receipt.

We recommended that Management:

(a) require the submission of all supporting documents of transactions
identified above to the COA Audit Team to avoid suspension of said
transactions; and

(b) ensure that complete supporting documents be attached in future
disbursements in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001
to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management require an allocation of one percent
(1%} for Local Couneils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) Programs in the
Municipality’s Annual Budget for the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management:

(a.) immediately create a committee to facilitate the updating and revision of
the SWMP for such plan to be approved by the Commission and eventually
be implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and

(b.) expedite the creation of solid waste management board that will be in
charge of the operation of the SWMP.

‘We recommended that Management ensure that all projects identified under
the 20% Economic Development Fund be implemented within the budget year.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better
ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of
structuring the GAD Focal Point System. Roles and responsibilities could have been
acted upon, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS must
submit the agency's GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) fo the Provincial Planning and
Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see
to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial
government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional
Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval.
Actual GAD projects and activities be also aligned with the GAD plan per COA Circular
14-001 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

Lastly, the 3% allotment for GAD budget be followed to maximize GAD projects
and activities.

We recommended that the Management require the Local Development Council
(LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in
Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, or whoever is in custody thereof, to submit without
undue delay to the Office of the Auditor the paid disbursement vouchers/payrolls within
the due date to enable us to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the trust collections due to other government
agencies. Prompt remittance of trust collections fo other government agencies concerned
must be observed to allow the latter of the timely use of these resources for productive
economic programs and activities. Trace ouf the records of Due to Other GOCCs
accounts without supporting schedule.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning and
Development Officer provide the Auditor af the beginning of the year a list of all on-
going and to be implemented PPAs during the year to monitor the status of
implementation of each infrastructure project.

We reiterated compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous
audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of
LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to prepare the
updated bank reconciliation statements for all funds and submit the same to COA for
verification. The same will be used as basis to ascertain and validate the cash in bank
balances recorded in the Financial Statements for CY 2016.

We recommended that management require the General Services Officer to
submit coples of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting
documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review
of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA4 TAS.

We recommended that all collectors be required to turn-over daily and intact all
their collections for the day fo the liguidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer and deposit
intact all collections daily or the next banking day to authorized depository bank
pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAs.

We also recommended to management to immediately prepare and send demand
letters to the former and current Accountable Officers/Collecting Clerks requiring them
to fidly settle/turn-over the balance of their accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipality enact a Municipal Ordinance establishing
the Solid Waste Management Board, prepare Solid Waste Management Plan of the
Maunicipality, implement no segregation, no collection policy, and stop the practice of
open burning of garbage.
The management will strictly comply with the provisions of NGAS Manual and Section 79 of PD 1445.

We reiterated  our            recommendation  that the  Municipal  Mayor:  Require the  Inventory  Committee  to  complete the  physical count  of all  inventory items  and submit the  corresponding  Physical  Inventory  Report, RPCI  and RPCPPE,  semestral and  annual,        respectively;  Instruct the  Municipal  Accountant and  the Municipal  Treasurer to  reconcile the  existing  discrepancy  between the  balances per  book and  RPCPPE;  Require the  Municipal  Accountant to  see to it that  reconciliation  is made between  balances per  inventory  reports and  accounting  records for a  fair  presentation of  the affected  account        balances in the  financial  statements. Resort to the  withholding of  salaries of  those          accountable  officers and  employees who  still fail or  persistently  refuse to  settle/liquidat  e their cash  advances;    Exert extra  efforts to  recover  outstanding  cash advances  from           Accountable  Officers who  are no longer  in the service  nor connected  with the  municipality;   Require  strictl y an  Office  Clearance from  Money and  Property  accountabilitie  s, pursuant to  Sections 200  and 256, Vol. I  of the  Government  Accounting and  Auditing Manual  from all  personnel who  seek  retirement,  resignation,    transfer or  claim for death  benefits in  order that all  accountabilitie  s could be  appropriately  settled; and   Instruct the  Municipal  Accountant to  strengthen  internal  control by  observing  Section 89 of  PD No. 1445 and  pertinent  provisions of  COA Circular  No. 97-002  dated February  10, 1997 and  make necessary  adjustments on  erroneous  entries made to  eliminate the  abnormal  balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2015-002 dated March 9, 2015 and 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.  The Accountant should immediately submit the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules.
observations.managementAction: The financial statements were submitted after the due date

observations.recommendation:
We request for the immediate submission of the required supporting documents with complete data, otherwise the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payment as jointly and severally liable of the amount disbursed.

We recommend strict compliance with the aforementioned law in the disbursement of government funds.  The concerned municipal officials should refrain from approving disbursements unless duly supported with complete documentation. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should see to it that all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim before payments are made to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We recommend that management cause the refund by concerned non-health professional/staff of the honoraria received in the total amount of P8,000.00. Henceforth, strict compliance with the utilization guidelines of the Philhealth Capitation Fund is hereby enjoined.

Accountable officers with outstanding balances be required to immediately submit their liquidation report/documents pursuant to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

The Accounting Section be required to record the liquidation documents submitted by the concerned Accountable Officers so that the corresponding expense or related accounts will be booked up.

Management should impose sanctions on Accountable Officers who fail to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period thru the withholding of their salaries as provided under Section 5.1.3 of the above Circular, especially those with unliquidated balances totaling more than P50,000.00 and those with cash advances which have remained outstanding for more than one (1) year.

Management should strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend Management to intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/special education tax collection.  The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.

We reiterate our recommendation that an inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE and a report therein promptly prepared. In addition, when the physical inventory is already available, it shall be reconciled with the (a) PPE ledger card, (b) control accounts and (c) property cards. If discrepancies exist, it should be immediately verified and necessary adjustments in the books then prepared to ensure fairness of the accounts presentation in the financial statements. Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly observe the provisions of Sec. 119, 120 and 124, Vol. 1of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

We reiterate our recommendation that management should exert efforts to provide for the depreciation of PPE accounts in order to present accurate and reliable balances of fixed assets and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterate our recommendation that municipal officials concerned should initiate the titling of the parcels of land owned by the LGU in order to validate the ownership thereof.  Further, the Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers for the land account and exert effort in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to locate the source documents pertaining to the acquisition thereof for titling and valuation purposes.

Management should determine the employees responsible for payment of VECO bills and charge the cost of penalties and surcharges to the personal account of the persons found negligent. Henceforth, due dates of electricity bills should be properly monitored and payments be made on time to avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the P5,000.00/ P7,000.00/ P10,000.00/ P13,000.00/ P23,000.00 excess amount of PEI received, otherwise all those involved who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable therefore.

We recommend that management cause the refund by concerned BAC and TWG members the honoraria received in the total amount of P744,000.00 after the Notice of Disallowance shall have become final and executory.  Henceforth, strict compliance with Budget Circular Nos. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 and 2007-3, dated November 29, 2007 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 is hereby enjoined.

We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the P500/ P6,000/ P16,000/ P21,000/ P31,000 excess amount of PEI received, otherwise all those involved who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable therefore.

A Notice of Disallowance will be issued in this regard.

We recommend that the management should require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and prepare the respective financial statements of each special account in the General Fund to provide them adequate information in determining the profitability and viability of operating these economic enterprises.

We recommend that the official/s concerned shall assume as personal liability the penalty imposed by VECO on the late payment of electric bill and refund the Municipality of the same amount of penalty payment.  Hereafter, due diligence must be observed in monitoring due dates of regular payables to avoid incurring monetary penalties/surcharges.

Adjusting entries should be prepared to close completed public infrastructures to government equity account.  The public infrastructures should be recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructures (RPI).

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should be required to submit the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and the tax due at the start of each year to the Municipal Accountant as basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables in order to present reliable and accurate receivable and deferred income account balances as well as determine tax collection efficiency and effectiveness of the municipal government.
We recommended to management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to submit long overdue reports, in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: Some lacking reports were already submitted.  Delay in submission is still observed thus reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended to management to require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances.  Adhere  to the provisions on  the granting and utilization of cash advances  as prescribed by Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Some accountable persons with outstanding cash advances were no longer connected with the LGU.  Management to send demand letters to concerned personnel.  Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We are recommending to management to maintain subsidiary ledgers and prepare supporting schedules to support recorded transactions.  Regularly conduct physical inventory of its properties at least once a year to determine existence and physical condition of the assets.
observations.reason: Depreciation expense was already recognized.  Preparation of subsidiary ledgers and the corresponding schedule is on-going. Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We are recommending to management to instruct the Accountant  and the LDRRMO to adhere to the existing guidelines on the accounting and  reporting of the  5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF). Disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The unspent 5% LDRRMF  was transferred to the TF.  Management failed to strictly observed frequency of preparation and submission of the required report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  to management to maintain subsidiary ledgers and prepare supporting schedules to support recorded transactions.  Regularly conduct physical inventory of its properties at least once a year to determine existence and physical condition of the assets.  Recognize depreciation expense to all the depreciable property of the municipality.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel cause the delay in the submission of the report.  This is reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management  to comply strictly with the existing CSC policy, rules and regulations on rewarding benefits under the PRAISE program to ensure its propriety and regularity.
observations.reason: Management did not release any PRAISE benefit this year.  A disallowance was issued for the transaction which is under appeal by the LGU .

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management to reconcile and correct the errors that affect the reliability of the Reciprocal Accounts in all transactions affecting the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds; Subsidy from Other Funds and Subsidy to Other Funds accounts, in accordance with the NGAS provisions.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office has already made some adjustments on the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to adhere to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations governing the deposits of government funds. The Treasurer shall be instructed to transfer the remaining balance of deposit with the private bank to the LBP or DBP  or to  obtain authority from the DOF/BLGF and comply with the requirements set by the circular 	if management chooses to maintain a deposit other than the AGDB.

We recommended to management to require the members of the PLEB to refund the excess of per diem granted to them. Henceforth, adhere to the rate set by the existing regulation in disbursement of PLEB per diems. Include in the annual budget of the municipality an appropriation for the maintenance and operations of the PLEB.
observations.reason: Some members has yet to make the refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to  instruct the GSO and the accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for the proper recording of purchases and utilization  of supplies and drugs and medicines.
We recommended to management that the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay be observed.
observations.reason: Management  verifies/monitors the net take home pay of employees if the latter apply for loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management  to insure all its properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS as  required by AO No. 33 of 1987 to indemnify local government against loss or damage.
observations.reason: Only vehicles were insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to require the BAC of the municipality to adhere to the preparation of the procurement monitoring report, duly approved by the Local Chief Executive  and its submission to GPPB.

The Local Chief Executive should require the concerned office and officials to see to it that prioritized projects under the 20% development fund are implemented as planned, in order to attain the desired outcomes of the projects and to enable the constituents to enjoy their full benefits.
observations.reason: Management deferred the release of another fund transfer to the barangays due to non liquidation of previously granted  fund transfers contributing to the underutilization of the fund.    Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management  instruct the concerned office to submit the returned contracts and purchase orders as well as all contracts awarded in CY 2014 to facilitate legal and auditorial review. Adhere to the submission of copies of contracts and purchase  orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with its supporting documents forming parts thereof and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of contracts is still recurring.

observations.managementAction: Augmentation in workforce to facilitate inventory taking of all PPEs as well as to organize an inventory team for efficient conduct of physical count of PPE..
observations.reason:
Physical count  was conducted by management however it was incomplete hence, an  observation  was issued.

Restated in Part 11 of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination - with the Municipal Engineer to immediately- identify the completed projects still included under Construction in Progress accounts;

b. The Municipal Accountant to transfer the concerned accounts to their respective Property, Plant and Equipment accounts; and

c. Reflect the corresponding Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation for the assets, to reflect the appropriate amounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Immediate recording in the books of the transfer of completed projects to its appropriate accounts. However, some project in the CIP accounts were not completed.
observations.reason:
Completed projects were transferred to its proper account,

accounts were not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the concerned members of the Disposal Committee, effect the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with COA Circular 92-386, in order to generate additional funds, if found valuable and to avoid the further deterioration of the unserviceable properties due to exposure to natural elements and possible loss due to theft and loss of value.
observations.managementAction: Gather relevant documents in order to conduct disposal of unserviceable properties. 2014 Undergo training relative to disposal.
observations.reason:
The inadequacy of documents necessary to conduct disposal.

The lack of training for concemed member of the disposal committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct:

1. the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to observe the proper procedures in procurement transactions and ensure that all necessary documents are submitted to the Accounting Section on time to avoid the occurrence of the same error in the future;

2. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements to reflect the correct balances of the accounts; and

3. the LGU officials concerned to conform with the proper schedules in the closing of the books of accounts as of year-end and avoid delays.
observations.managementAction: Necessary accounting entries were recorded
observations.reason:
AAR 2015

The recommendations 2015 were complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. remit the collection to the Municipal Health Office thru its concerned officers, to immediately remit collections from the Municipal Lying Clinic to the Municipal Treasurer for proper issuance of official receipts and recording,

b. direct the concerned officers of the Municipal Health Office to maintain records of services rendered by the Municipal Hospital, thru a logbook or a registry, to be reconciled on a regular basis, with the fees collected by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer;

c. direct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit records and reports of collections to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition of income; and

d. issue a memorandum to all department heads of the LGU, reiterating the adherence to the Revised Local Revenue Code, relative to the objective of the LGU of improving its income-generating capacity; and e. issue an order, to be disseminated orally and in writing to the constituents of the LGU, that any fees and charges imposed by the LGU, be directly paid and remitted to the Municipal Treasurer for proper issuance of an official receipt.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Office still did not remit the collection citing differences in the existing standard fees between the Municipal Ordinance and from what was published in the newspaper.
observations.reason: The Municipal Health Officer did not remit the issuance of the official receipt nor recorded as income.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Mayor:

1. direct the responsible officials to conduct the physical count of all its Inventories on a regular basis; 2. direct the responsible official to maintain inventory records of each type of inventory, aside from the subsidiary ledgers maintained in the Accounting Section; and 3. direct the personnel responsible for the various inventory accounts to require end-users to acknowledge the receipt of the inventories requested and require the adequate monitoring of issued and balances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Management - prepared and submitted the Report on Physical Count Inventories for accountable forms.
observations.reason: Only the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories for the accountable forms were submitted by management. But for the rest of the inventories on hand as of December 31, 2016 none was given.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

a. the Municipal Treasurer to increase the collection efficiency, which is not in harmony with the mandate of RA 7160 to develop self-reliant LGUs, depriving the Municipality and its constituents of the funds necessary for the delivery of public services.

b. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that receivables for RPT and SET be set-up at the beginning of each year to determine the amount collectible; c. Municipal Assessor to submit at the soonest possible time, an updated list of real properties, with the names of their owners, and the assessment of the taxes for the said properties, to serve as basis for the imposition of said taxes; and d. involvement of the Punong Barangays and other barangay officials in the campaign for the collection of real property taxes or hold barangay assemblies to disseminate information.
observations.managementAction: Despite effort of the Municipal Treasurer to increase the collection efficiency, which is not in harmony with the mandate of RA 7160 to develop self-reliant LGUs, depriving the Municipality and its constituents of the funds necessary for the delivery of public services.
observations.reason:
AAR v 2015

Real Property Tax Receivables(RPT) / Special Education Tax(SET) Receivables for 2016 were not recorded at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to launch an extensive collection campaign for real property taxes, covering all the barangays necessary for the delivery of public services. b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that receivables for RPT and SET be set-up at the beginning of each year to determine the amount collectible; c. Municipal Assessor to submit at the soonest possible time, an updated list of real properties, with the names of their owners, and the assessment of the taxes for the said properties, to serve as basis for the imposition of said taxes; and d. involvement of the Punong Barangays and other barangay officials in the campaign for the collection of real property taxes or hold barangay assemblies to disseminate information.
observations.managementAction: Immense effort was done by the Municipal Treasurer. Out of the beginning balance of P4,263,227.11 of CY 2016 Only P1,017,814.80 was collected or 24%.
observations.reason:
AAR v 2015

Out of the beginning balance of P4,263,227.11 of CY 2016 Only P1,017,814.80 was collected or 24%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the immediate reconciliation of the corresponding cash balances maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure correctness and reliability of the reported cash balances as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly reconciliation of the treasury and the accounting records were conducted at year end, hence same observation was issued.
observations.reason: Discrepancy on the records between the treasurer’s cash book and the record of the accounting was observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. stop the granting of additional cash advances to employees with previous unliquidated cash advances;

b. demand immediate liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances based on the rules set forth in the above-mentioned provisions to compel them to liquidate their outstanding balances; and

c. strengthen the regular monitoring of cash advances observing the rules on the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same to ensure that funds are utilized timely and for the purpose to which they were intended.
observations.managementAction:
Management adopted measures to ensure liquidation of cash advances.

As of December 31, 2016 the total unliquidated cash advances was P198,363.03.
Necessary accounting journal entries were recorded to adjust the amount, Sent notices to payees who have unclaimed checks.
observations.reason:
Similar omission was noted during the audit year thus, an audit observation was issued.

Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Section and other concemmed LGU Offices to plan and implement programs and projects from the 20% Economic Development Plan as continuing appropriation, in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations to enable the Municipality to fully maximize the utilization of the fund for projects and programs towards socio-economic development and environmental management.
observations.managementAction: Projects under the fund for were partially implemented.

AAR v 2015

A total of fourteen (14) projects were not implemented for projects under the CY 2016 EDF appropriation and one project under the continuing appropriation did not push through.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to initiate the succeeding programs to put the initial outputs of the CLUP into use by starting with the evaluation of the prepared Phase 1 by the Technical Working Group to determine progress and accomplishments; b. Conduct participatory assessments and consultations with the various stakeholders of the LGU, including barangay officials to determine the accuracy of the data included in the CLUP for further analysis; c. Determine the practicability of pursuing Phase 2 of the CLUP preparation and implementation without the need of a private consultant; and d. Be guided by the principles of economy, efficiency and effectiveness in the conduct of any government program or project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget appropriation 5% for the Gender and Development (GAD) programs/activities as required under the above-stated circular. If the budget is incorporated or streamiined in other offices of the LGU, the appropriation should be marked as GAD related activities for proper identification and monitoring; b. the Mayor to direct the Focal Point Person, to comply with the mandates of the above-mentioned provisions by preparing a GAD Plan to embrace all GAD activities for the year, and the accomplishment report at the end of the year that will prove compliance to said mandate; and c. the Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Point Person to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the above-stated joint circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all members of the BAC re-orient themselves of their functions provided for in the RIRR of RA No. 9184. Compliance to the RIRR of RA No. 9184 is the mandated duty of all the members, non-compliance or ignorance thereof are not valid excuses since the RIRR was already given wide national dissemination. The practice should not be committed again.

We also recommended that the BAC, Secretariat, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Mayor should be cautious in the preparation of the bidding documents and other attachments in the procurement of goods.

Require the immediate submission of the print outs of the PhilGEPS postings for the two biddings as well as letters of invitation to the Audit Team and the two other mandated observers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. attendances of two or more persons to any out-of-town and out-of-region seminars, trainings, conventions, etc. be discouraged. Delivery of efficient and effective public management and disbursement of government funds, hence, prudence in the expenditures thereof, should be in mind at all times; b. the LGU Officials be reminded that not all letters of invitation to seminars, trainings, conventions, etc. are mandatory. Hence, priority should be given to those that are useful and significant in relation o the affairs of the LGU; and c. Management to strictly adhere to the allowable charges of per diem and costs of participation to seminars, conventions, etc. as prescribed under Executive Order (EO) No. 298 and National Budget Circular (NBC) No. 486 dated March 26, 2003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor: (a) direct officers and employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who failed to comply with the requirement; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advance and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; (c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances; and (d) take legal actions against previous employees with still unliquidated cash advance who are no longer in the government service.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured compliance of the audit recommendation.
